title: Fix: recovery .btn-primary has width:auto but login-page .btn-primary has no width — CSS divergence (from ePDS-aq2.5)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.5 found: The fac3f8d commit changed .btn-primary in recovery.ts to width:auto (line 675), but login-page.ts has no width property on .btn-primary at all (line 583-603). This is a CSS divergence between the two files.

Evidence:
- recovery.ts line 675: width: auto; (inside .btn-primary rule)
- login-page.ts: no width property on .btn-primary

In login-page.ts, the button is inside .otp-actions (flex column, align-items: flex-end) which naturally sizes it to content. In recovery.ts, the button is inside .form-actions (flex row, justify-content: space-between) where width:auto is correct to prevent it from stretching.

The divergence is intentional for the form-actions layout, but it means the CSS is no longer 'identical to login-page.ts' as the spec requires. This should be documented or the login-page.ts should also adopt width:auto for consistency.

Fix: Either document why width:auto is needed in recovery but not login-page, or add width:auto to login-page.ts .btn-primary as well (since inline-flex with auto width is the correct default for a button that should size to its content).

title: Doc comment in complete.ts has duplicate step label 5c and wrong step reference in comment 6
description: Review of 0962a39 found: The file-level JSDoc block in complete.ts (lines 18-20) has two entries labeled '5c': one for 'Existing user, needs consent' and one for 'Existing user, no consent needed'. The second should be '5d'. Additionally, line 20 says 'only for 5a random and 5d paths' but the inline comment at line 148 still says 'Step 5b: Redirect to consent screen' (should be 5c). These mismatches make the flow numbering inconsistent and confusing for future maintainers. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts lines 18-20 and line 148.

title: Fix: CSP img-src only allows AUTH_LOGO_URL origin, not client logo_uri origin (from ePDS-wae.10)
description: In index.ts (line 51-56), the CSP img-src is extended with the AUTH_LOGO_URL origin. However, the login page also renders client logo images from b.logo_uri (OAuth client metadata, line 224). If a client's logo_uri is on a different origin than AUTH_LOGO_URL, the image will be blocked by CSP. The client_id origin is added to img-src (lines 58-67) but the logo_uri may be on a CDN or different domain than the client_id URL. Fix: also fetch the logo_uri from client metadata in the CSP middleware and add its origin to img-src, or add it dynamically per-request when resolving client metadata.

title: Epic: Handle selection during signup — let new users choose their handle instead of getting a random one
description:
## Goal
New users should choose their own handle during signup instead of receiving a random one (e.g. a1b2c3.pds.example.com).

## Context
Currently, when a new user completes OTP verification, /auth/complete signs a callback and redirects to pds-core's epds-callback, which calls generateRandomHandle() and creates the account. Users can only change their handle post-signup via account settings.

## New Flow
After OTP verification, new users are redirected to a new /auth/choose-handle page where they pick a handle. The chosen handle is included in the HMAC-signed callback URL and used by epds-callback for account creation.

## Key Constraints
- No DB schema changes — handle flows through the signed callback URL
- HMAC payload in signCallback()/verifyCallback() must be extended to include handle (currently only signs 4 fields)
- complete.ts must NOT delete auth_flow or clear cookie when redirecting to choose-handle (defer cleanup to POST handler)
- New /_internal/check-handle endpoint must return only { exists: boolean } (not email)
- Handle validation: 3-20 chars, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, reserved handle blocklist
- Availability check endpoint must be session-gated with tighter rate limit
- Handle collision at createAccount() time must redirect back to picker, not 500

## Success Criteria
A new user signing up sees a handle picker page, can check availability in real-time, and their chosen handle is used for account creation. Existing user login flow is completely unaffected.
close_reason: a2f1708 all 11 tasks closed, 208 tests passing, review fixes applied

title: Extend HMAC callback signing to include optional handle parameter
description:
## Files
- packages/shared/src/crypto.ts (modify)

## What to do
1. Add optional `handle` field to the `CallbackParams` interface:
   ```ts
   export interface CallbackParams {
     request_uri: string
     email: string
     approved: string
     new_account: string
     handle?: string  // NEW — only set for new account creation with chosen handle
   }
   ```

2. In `signCallback()`, add handle to the HMAC payload. Use empty string as sentinel when absent so existing flows still produce valid signatures:
   ```ts
   const payload = [
     params.request_uri,
     params.email,
     params.approved,
     params.new_account,
     params.handle ?? '',  // empty string when absent
     ts,
   ].join('\n')
   ```

3. In `verifyCallback()`, make the same change to payload reconstruction:
   - Read `handle` from the query params (it may be undefined/absent)
   - Include `params.handle ?? ''` in the payload array at the same position

4. Update the `verifyCallback()` return type or the params extraction to also return the handle if present, so the caller (epds-callback) can read it.

## Don't
- Don't change the order of existing fields in the payload (request_uri, email, approved, new_account must stay in their current positions)
- Don't make handle required — it must be optional for backward compatibility with existing-user flows
- Don't modify any other functions in crypto.ts (generateRandomHandle, escapeHtml, etc.)
close_reason: 09e34fb Extend HMAC callback signing to include optional handle parameter

title: Fix: PDS API failure shows misleading 'handle taken' error
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)

## What to do
In the POST `/auth/choose-handle` handler, when the HTTP call to `/_internal/check-handle` returns a non-OK status (e.g., 403 from misconfigured EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET, or 500 from PDS error), the code falls through to the `if (!handleAvailable)` branch and shows 'That handle is already taken.' This is misleading — the real problem is a PDS API failure.

### Current behavior:
```
fetch /_internal/check-handle → 403 Forbidden
→ handleAvailable stays false
→ shows 'That handle is already taken'
```

### Expected behavior:
```
fetch /_internal/check-handle → 403 Forbidden
→ detect non-OK response
→ shows 'Could not verify handle availability. Please try again.'
```

### Fix:
In the POST handler, after the fetch to `/_internal/check-handle`, check `checkRes.ok` explicitly. If it's not OK, re-render the page with a distinct error message like 'Could not verify handle availability. Please try again.' and return early — don't fall through to the 'handle taken' branch.

Apply the same fix to the `GET /api/check-handle` endpoint — if the PDS returns non-OK, return `{ error: 'service_unavailable' }` instead of `{ available: false }`.

## Don't
- Don't change the happy path (PDS returns 200 with { exists: true/false })
- Don't change the catch block (network errors already have their own message)
- Don't modify the GET /auth/choose-handle handler
close_reason: 5565ecf fix: PDS API failure now shows 'Could not verify handle availability' instead of misleading 'handle taken' error; GET /api/check-handle returns service_unavailable on non-OK PDS response

title: Fix: Send only handle local part through callback, append domain in pds-core
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)
- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (modify)

## What to do
Currently the full handle (e.g., `alice.pds.example.com`) is sent through the HMAC-signed callback URL. Instead, send only the local part (`alice`) and let pds-core append its own trusted `handleDomain`. This eliminates any possibility of domain mismatch.

### Change 1: choose-handle.ts POST handler
In the POST handler, when calling `signCallback()`, change:
```ts
// Before:
handle: fullHandle  // e.g., 'alice.pds.example.com'

// After:
handle: localPart   // e.g., 'alice'
```

The `localPart` is already available — it's `req.body.handle` after lowercasing and trimming. The `fullHandle` (`${localPart}.${handleDomain}`) is still used for the availability check against the PDS, but only the local part goes into the signed callback.

### Change 2: pds-core/index.ts epds-callback handler
Where the chosen handle is read from the verified callback params, construct the full handle from the local part + the PDS's own handleDomain:
```ts
// Before:
const chosenHandle = callbackVerification.handle  // 'alice.pds.example.com'

// After:
const chosenHandleLocal = callbackVerification.handle  // 'alice'
const chosenHandle = chosenHandleLocal ? `${chosenHandleLocal}.${handleDomain}` : undefined
```

Also update the defense-in-depth validation to validate just the local part (which is what it already does — it splits on '.' and checks the first segment). Since we're now receiving only the local part, simplify the validation:
```ts
if (chosenHandleLocal && !/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{1,18}[a-z0-9]$/.test(chosenHandleLocal)) {
  // invalid format
}
```

No need to split on '.' anymore since there's no domain to strip.

## Don't
- Don't change the HMAC signing/verification logic in crypto.ts
- Don't change the availability check logic (that still uses the full handle to query the PDS)
- Don't change the GET handler or the /api/check-handle endpoint
- Don't change how the handle is displayed on the UI
close_reason: 49ea292 fix: send only handle local part through callback, append domain in pds-core

title: Add /_internal/check-handle endpoint to pds-core
close_reason: bb26d43 Add /_internal/check-handle endpoint to pds-core

title: Modify complete.ts to redirect new users to /auth/choose-handle
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts (modify)

## What to do
Change the new-user path in the `/auth/complete` route so that instead of immediately signing the callback and redirecting to epds-callback, it redirects to `/auth/choose-handle`.

### Current behavior (new user, no PDS account):
The route:
1. Looks up auth_flow from cookie
2. Gets email from better-auth session
3. Checks PDS for existing account — not found
4. Signs the HMAC callback URL
5. Deletes auth_flow row
6. Clears epds_auth_flow cookie
7. Redirects to epds-callback

### New behavior (new user, no PDS account):
The route:
1. Looks up auth_flow from cookie
2. Gets email from better-auth session
3. Checks PDS for existing account — not found
4. **Redirects to `/auth/choose-handle`** — that's it

**CRITICAL: Do NOT call `ctx.db.deleteAuthFlow(flowId)` or `res.clearCookie(AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE)` for this path.** The auth_flow row and cookie must remain intact so the choose-handle page can read them. The POST /auth/choose-handle handler will do the cleanup later (same deferred-cleanup pattern as consent.ts).

### Existing-user behavior:
Completely unchanged. Existing users (PDS account found) still go through the consent/skip-consent flow as before.

### How to identify the branch:
Look for the code path where `isNewAccount` is true (or where the PDS account lookup returns null/undefined for the email). That's the branch to modify. The code currently calls `signCallback()` and `res.redirect()` to epds-callback — replace that with a simple `res.redirect('/auth/choose-handle')`.

## Don't
- Don't modify the existing-user flow (consent page, skip-consent, etc.)
- Don't delete the auth_flow row or clear the cookie for the new-user redirect
- Don't add any new dependencies
- Don't change how the auth_flow cookie is set (that happens in login-page.ts)
close_reason: 7db1992 Redirect new users to /auth/choose-handle in complete.ts

title: Create choose-handle route with handle picker page, POST handler, and availability check API
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a new route file that exports `createChooseHandleRouter(ctx)` returning an Express Router with three handlers.

### Handler 1: GET /auth/choose-handle — Render handle picker page

**Guards (must all pass, return error page if not):**
- Read `epds_auth_flow` cookie → look up auth_flow row via `ctx.db.getAuthFlow(flowId)` → if missing/expired, return 400 error page ('Session expired, please start over')
- Get better-auth session (same pattern as complete.ts — call better-auth session endpoint internally or use `ctx.auth`) → if no session, return 401 error page
- Check if PDS account already exists for this email via `getDidByEmail()` helper (same as complete.ts uses) → if account exists, redirect to `/auth/complete` (user shouldn't be here)

**Render:**
Call a `renderChooseHandlePage(handleDomain, error?)` template function that returns HTML with:
- Heading: 'Choose your handle'
- Subtext: 'Your handle is your public username on the AT Protocol network.'
- Text input for local part (id='handle-input', name='handle', placeholder='yourname', autocomplete='off')
- Static suffix text: `.{handleDomain}` displayed next to the input
- Status area (id='handle-status') for availability feedback
- Submit button (id='submit-btn', initially disabled)
- Form POSTs to `/auth/choose-handle`
- If `error` param is provided (e.g., from query string ?error=handle_taken), show it as an error message

**Client-side JavaScript (inline in the page):**
- On input, lowercase the value, strip invalid chars
- Validate with regex: `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{1,18}[a-z0-9]$/` (3-20 chars)
- If invalid, show format error in status area, disable submit
- If valid, debounce 500ms, then fetch `GET /api/check-handle?handle=${value}`
- While fetching, show 'Checking...' spinner text in status area
- On response: if `available: true`, show green '✓ Available!' + enable submit. If `available: false`, show red '✗ Already taken' + disable submit. If `error`, show the error message.
- Submit button stays disabled until handle is valid AND available

**Reserved handle blocklist (check client-side AND server-side):**
`admin, support, help, abuse, postmaster, root, system, moderator, www, mail, ftp, api, auth, oauth, account, settings, security, info, contact, noreply, no-reply`

**Styling:** Match existing auth-service page styles. Look at how `renderLoginPage()` in login-page.ts and `renderSettingsPage()` in account-settings.ts are styled. Use the same CSS patterns (card layout, input styles, button styles). The page should look like it belongs in the same auth flow.

### Handler 2: POST /auth/choose-handle — Validate, sign callback, redirect

**Guards:** Same as GET (cookie + session + new-account check)

**Logic:**
1. Read `req.body.handle` (local part only, from form submission)
2. Lowercase and trim it
3. Validate format with regex `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{1,18}[a-z0-9]$/`
4. Check against reserved handle blocklist
5. If invalid → re-render page with error
6. Construct full handle: `${localPart}.${handleDomain}` where handleDomain comes from `ctx.config.pdsHostname`
7. Check availability: HTTP GET to `${ctx.config.pdsInternalUrl}/_internal/check-handle?handle=${fullHandle}` with `x-internal-secret` header
8. If taken → re-render page with error 'That handle is already taken'
9. If available:
   a. Read flow context: `flow.requestUri` from auth_flow row, `email` from better-auth session
   b. Call `signCallback(ctx.config.epdsCallbackSecret, { request_uri: flow.requestUri, email, approved: '1', new_account: '1', handle: fullHandle })`
   c. Delete auth_flow row: `ctx.db.deleteAuthFlow(flowId)`
   d. Clear cookie: `res.clearCookie('epds_auth_flow')`
   e. Redirect to the signed callback URL

**Import signCallback from `@certified-app/shared`** — same import pattern as complete.ts and consent.ts.

### Handler 3: GET /api/check-handle — JSON availability endpoint

**Guards:**
- Require active better-auth session (return 401 JSON if not)
- Apply a tighter rate limit if possible (or document that the global rate limit applies)

**Logic:**
1. Read `req.query.handle` as string (local part only)
2. Validate format with same regex
3. Check against reserved blocklist
4. If invalid → return `{ error: 'invalid_format' }`
5. If reserved → return `{ error: 'reserved', available: false }`
6. Construct full handle: `${localPart}.${handleDomain}`
7. HTTP GET to `${ctx.config.pdsInternalUrl}/_internal/check-handle?handle=${fullHandle}` with `x-internal-secret` header
8. If PDS returns `{ exists: true }` → return `{ available: false, handle: fullHandle }`
9. If PDS returns `{ exists: false }` → return `{ available: true, handle: fullHandle }`

### Context object
The route receives an `AuthServiceContext` (ctx) which has:
- `ctx.config.pdsHostname` — the PDS domain (e.g., 'pds.example.com')
- `ctx.config.pdsInternalUrl` — internal URL for PDS (e.g., 'http://core:3000')
- `ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl` — public URL for PDS
- `ctx.config.epdsCallbackSecret` — HMAC secret for signing callbacks
- `ctx.config.internalSecret` — secret for x-internal-secret header
- `ctx.db` — database instance with getAuthFlow(), deleteAuthFlow()
- `ctx.auth` — better-auth instance

Look at how complete.ts and consent.ts access these to follow the same patterns.

## Don't
- Don't add any new npm dependencies
- Don't modify any existing route files (complete.ts, consent.ts, etc.)
- Don't add a DB migration or schema change
- Don't use default exports — use named export `createChooseHandleRouter`
close_reason: a9ea269 Add choose-handle route with handle picker page, POST handler, and availability check API

title: Fix: CSRF token missing from choose-handle form
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)

## What to do
The choose-handle form is missing the CSRF hidden input. The global CSRF middleware rejects every POST with 403.

### Step 1: Pass csrfToken to the template in the GET handler
In the GET `/auth/choose-handle` handler, when calling `renderChooseHandlePage()`, pass `csrfToken: res.locals.csrfToken` as part of the options.

### Step 2: Pass csrfToken to the template in the POST handler (error re-render)
When the POST handler re-renders the page on validation errors (invalid format, reserved handle, handle taken), also pass `csrfToken: res.locals.csrfToken`.

### Step 3: Update renderChooseHandlePage to accept and render csrfToken
Add `csrfToken?: string` to the function's options/params. Inside the `<form>` tag, add:
```html
<input type="hidden" name="csrf" value="${escapeHtml(csrfToken || '')}" />
```
This must be inside the `<form method="POST" action="/auth/choose-handle">` element, before the other inputs.

### Reference
Look at how other forms in the codebase include the CSRF token:
- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/consent.ts` — the consent form has `<input type="hidden" name="csrf" value="${escapeHtml(csrfToken)}">`
- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/account-settings.ts` — same pattern

Follow the exact same pattern.

## Don't
- Don't modify the CSRF middleware itself
- Don't remove or change any other form fields
- Don't change the POST handler logic (validation, signing, redirect)
close_reason: 5565ecf fix: add CSRF token hidden input to choose-handle form

title: Fix: Handle collision redirect uses Docker-internal URL instead of public URL
description:
## Files
- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (modify)

## What to do
The handle collision redirect in the epds-callback handler uses `process.env.AUTH_SERVICE_URL || 'http://auth:3001'` which is a Docker-internal URL unreachable from the user's browser.

### Current code (broken):
```ts
const authServiceUrl = process.env.AUTH_SERVICE_URL || 'http://auth:3001'
res.redirect(`${authServiceUrl}/auth/choose-handle?error=handle_taken`)
```

### Fix:
Use the same public auth URL pattern used elsewhere in this file. Look for how `authHostname` is derived (it should be something like `process.env.AUTH_HOSTNAME || 'auth.localhost'`). Then:
```ts
res.redirect(303, `https://${authHostname}/auth/choose-handle?error=handle_taken`)
```

Key changes:
1. Use `authHostname` (public URL) instead of `AUTH_SERVICE_URL` (internal URL)
2. Add `303` status code (matches all other redirects in this file — see I-4 fix below)
3. Remove the `AUTH_SERVICE_URL` reference entirely since it's not needed

### Also fix I-4 (303 status code):
The redirect should use status 303 (not the default 302) to match every other redirect in this file and prevent the browser from re-POSTing on back navigation. This is included in this task since it's the same line of code.

## Don't
- Don't add new environment variables
- Don't modify any other redirects in the file
- Don't change the handle collision detection logic
close_reason: 966723f fix: handle collision redirect uses public authHostname (303) instead of Docker-internal AUTH_SERVICE_URL

title: Plan adding passkey login support to ePDS auth service
description:
Add WebAuthn/FIDO2 passkey authentication as a login method in the auth service. This is a significant feature that needs its own design doc (docs/design/passkey-login.md) before implementation begins.

The design doc should cover:
- How passkeys fit into the existing OTP-based login flow (alternative to email OTP, not replacement)
- Registration flow: when/where users register passkeys (after first OTP login? dedicated settings page?)
- Authentication flow: how the auth service detects a user has passkeys and offers them
- Integration with better-auth (does better-auth have a passkey plugin, or do we use @simplewebauthn directly?)
- Storage: where passkey credentials are stored (SQLite via EpdsDb? better-auth's DB?)
- UI changes to login-page.ts: showing a 'Sign in with passkey' option
- Interaction with the two login flows (Flow 1: client passes login_hint, Flow 2: auth collects email)
- Security considerations: attestation, resident keys, user verification requirements

Reference: maearth-demo has a partial 2FA passkey implementation (src/lib/twofa.ts, src/app/api/twofa/passkey-*) using @simplewebauthn/server + @simplewebauthn/browser, but that's for 2FA verification after login, not as a primary login method. The approach here may differ.

First step: write the design doc. Implementation beads will be created from it.

title: Fix: tryRandomHandle fetch has no timeout — button stays disabled indefinitely on hung server (from ePDS-cg8)
description: Review of 4246a86 found: The fetch() call inside tryRandomHandle() (choose-handle.ts line 599) has no AbortSignal.timeout(), unlike the regular checkAvailability() which uses an AbortController for cancellation. If the server hangs, the random button remains disabled indefinitely with no way for the user to recover (they cannot click the button again because it is disabled). The fix is to add signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) to the fetch call in tryRandomHandle, and handle the TimeoutError in the catch block by re-enabling the button and showing an error. Evidence: choose-handle.ts line 599 vs line 515 (which uses currentAbort.signal).

title: Fix: consent-screen Scenario 4 uses 'authenticates via OTP through the demo client' but no PDS account exists (from ePDS-geiz)
description:
Review of ePDS-geiz found: consent-screen.feature Scenario 4 ('New user skips consent entirely') is:
  Given no PDS account exists for "newuser@example.com"
  When "newuser@example.com" authenticates via OTP through the demo client
  Then no consent screen is shown (account creation implies consent)
  And a PDS account is created
  And the browser is redirected to the demo client with a valid session

The 'no PDS account exists for' step (common.steps.ts line 131) sets world.testEmail to a unique email but does NOT set up a browser session or navigate anywhere.

The 'authenticates via OTP through the demo client' step (auth.steps.ts line 358) checks testEnv.mailpitPass and world.testEmail, then navigates to demoUrl and drives the full OAuth flow. This is correct.

However, the step does NOT wait for /welcome at the end (comment says 'Do NOT wait for /welcome — the flow may stop at /auth/consent'). For a NEW user, the flow should go directly to /welcome (no consent screen). But the step stops after clicking the OTP verify button without waiting for any URL.

The subsequent 'no consent screen is shown (account creation implies consent)' step (auth.steps.ts line 583) waits for **/welcome with a 30s timeout. This should work, but if the flow unexpectedly shows a consent screen, the timeout error message will be confusing.

This is a minor style issue — the step could be clearer about what it expects for new users vs returning users. Consider splitting into separate steps or adding a comment.
close_reason: 8a9e0ea clarify shared step comments for consent-screen Scenario 4

title: Security: CSS injection via AUTH_BRAND_COLOR / AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR / AUTH_PANEL_COLOR env vars (from ePDS-wae.10)
description:
## Problem

Review of ePDS-wae.10 (commit 9fee49a) found a CSS injection vulnerability.

The three color env vars are inserted directly into CSS custom property values inside a `style` attribute and a `<style>` block:

```ts
// index.ts line 242
rootStyleProps += `--color-primary:${escapeHtml(brandColor)};--color-primary-contrast:#fff;`
// index.ts line 245
rootStyleProps += `--color-panel:${escapeHtml(panelColor)};--color-panel-text:#fff;...`
// login-page.ts line 253
const bgColorStyle = bgColor
  ? `<style>body { background: ${escapeHtml(bgColor)} !important; }</style>`
  : ''
```

`escapeHtml()` only escapes HTML entities (`&`, `<`, `>`, `"`). It does NOT escape CSS-special characters: `;`, `}`, `(`, `)`, `url(`.

A malicious operator (or misconfigured deployment) setting:
```
AUTH_BRAND_COLOR=#3E7053; background: url(https://evil.com/track.gif)
AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR=red} body::after{content:url(https://evil.com/x)
AUTH_PANEL_COLOR=#3E7053; --color-panel-text: red
```

...would inject arbitrary CSS into the login page. For the `<style>` block (bgColorStyle), this is a full CSS injection — an attacker can exfiltrate form data via CSS attribute selectors + background-image requests.

## Evidence

- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 241-253
- packages/auth-service/src/index.ts lines 141-143
- packages/shared/src/html.ts — escapeHtml() only handles HTML entities, not CSS

## Fix

Add a CSS color validator before using these values. Accept only:
1. Hex colors: `/^#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$/`
2. Named CSS colors from a whitelist
3. `rgb()` / `hsl()` with numeric-only arguments validated by regex

Reject (log warning, fall back to default) any value that doesn't match. Apply this in `main()` in index.ts when reading env vars, before storing in config.
close_reason: 814f241 security: validate branding env vars to prevent CSS injection

The `renderOtpForm()` function uses `·` as a visual separator but there is no matching CSS rule. The login-page.ts file already defines this class — match its styling. Look at `packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts` to find the existing `.otp-links-dot` CSS rule and copy the same styles into `getBaseCSS()` in recovery.ts. Place it near the existing `.otp-links` rule. The rule should set the color to the muted text color variable used in the design (likely `var(--color-text-light)` or similar — match whatever login-page.ts uses). ## Don't - Don't change any HTML templates - Don't change login-page.ts - Don't change any other CSS rules in recovery.ts - Don't modify any other file

title: Fix: choose-handle GET shows arbitrary unvalidated error text from query param
description:
Review of the handle mode system found: in choose-handle.ts GET handler (lines 169-175), the error query parameter is shown to the user if it is not in the KNOWN_ERROR_MESSAGES allowlist:

  const rawError = req.query.error as string | undefined
  const error = rawError ? (KNOWN_ERROR_MESSAGES[rawError] ?? rawError) : undefined

If rawError is not 'handle_taken' (the only known value), the raw query string value is passed directly to renderChooseHandlePage() and displayed to the user. XSS is prevented by escapeHtml() in the template, but arbitrary text from the URL can be shown (e.g. ?error=Your+account+has+been+suspended).

While exploitation requires an active auth_flow cookie (session-gated), showing arbitrary URL-sourced text is bad practice and could be used for social engineering against users in an active signup flow.

The fix: replace the fallback to rawError with a generic 'An error occurred' message for unknown error codes, or simply ignore unknown error codes entirely:
  const error = rawError ? (KNOWN_ERROR_MESSAGES[rawError] ?? undefined) : undefined

Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts lines 169-175.

title: Fix: verifyOtp uses currentEmail which is empty string when OTP step is server-rendered (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: When initialStep='otp' (login_hint provided), the JS sets currentEmail = loginHint at line 1054. However, the form-verify-otp submit handler at line 1007 calls verifyOtp(currentEmail, otp). If the user somehow submits the form before the JS initialization block runs (e.g. very fast submit, or if the initialization is at the bottom of the script), currentEmail would be '' (its initial value at line 891). In practice the script runs synchronously so this is unlikely, but it is fragile. More critically: if the user navigates back to the OTP step via btn-back and then forward again, currentEmail is correctly set by showOtpStep(). But on the initial server-rendered OTP step, currentEmail is set from loginHint (line 1054) which is correct. The hidden input #otp-email also has the email value. The verifyOtp function should read from otpEmailInput.value as a fallback: var email = currentEmail || otpEmailInput.value;
close_reason: 1d3e269 fix: verifyOtp falls back to otpEmailInput.value when currentEmail is empty

title: Security: AUTH_LOGO_URL not validated — allows tracking pixel from arbitrary domain (from ePDS-wae.10)
description: ## Problem Review of ePDS-wae.10 (commit 9fee49a) found that AUTH_LOGO_URL is accepted without any URL validation. In packages/auth-service/src/index.ts: ```ts logoUrl: process.env.AUTH_LOGO_URL || undefined, ``` In packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts: ```ts const logoHtml = logoUrl ? `` : '' ``` And in packages/auth-service/src/index.ts CSP middleware: ```ts if (ctx.config.logoUrl) { const logoOrigin = new URL(ctx.config.logoUrl).origin if (logoOrigin && logoOrigin !== 'null') imgSrc += ` ${logoOrigin}` } ``` Issues: 1. No scheme validation — a `javascript:` URL would be placed in `src` (browsers block it for `` but it's still bad practice) 2. No hostname validation — an operator could accidentally set this to a tracking pixel URL (e.g. from a third-party analytics service), which would then be whitelisted in CSP and loaded on every login page view, leaking user visit data to that third party 3. The URL is accepted as-is from the environment without any sanitization beyond HTML-escaping ## Evidence - packages/auth-service/src/index.ts lines 51-56, 140 - packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 222-225 ## Fix In `main()` in index.ts, validate AUTH_LOGO_URL before storing: 1. Must be a valid URL (parseable by `new URL()`) 2. Must use `https:` scheme only 3. Log a warning and set to `undefined` if invalid This is operator-controlled so severity is lower than the CSS injection issue, but it's a defense-in-depth gap.

title: Fix: .btn-primary in OTP step is not full-width despite design intent (from ePDS-yzs)
description: Review of ePDS-yzs (OTP step button redesign) found that the 'Sign In with Certified' primary button in the OTP step (line 869) uses .btn-primary which is display:inline-flex with no width set. The .otp-actions container uses align-items:flex-end, so the button shrinks to its content width rather than spanning the full form width. The design intent (per ePDS-yzs: 'full-width primary') requires width:100% on the button. The email step's 'Continue with Email' button (line 839) has the same issue. Fix: add width:100% to .btn-primary, or add a .btn-full modifier class and apply it to both submit buttons.

title: Fix: .btn-primary not full-width in email step otp-actions (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The .otp-actions container (line 532) uses `align-items: flex-end` which right-aligns its children. The 'Continue with Email' button in the email step (line 756) is inside .otp-actions but .btn-primary has no `width: 100%` rule, so it renders as a right-aligned pill button rather than a full-width button.

Evidence:
- Line 532-538: .otp-actions has `align-items: flex-end` — children are right-aligned
- Line 583-603: .btn-primary has no `width: 100%`
- Line 756: `<button type="submit" class="btn-primary">Continue with Email</button>` inside .otp-actions
- The spec (ePDS-aq2.1) says: '.btn-primary: ... full width in otp-actions'

The OTP submit button (line 788) has the same issue. Both buttons should be full-width.

Fix: Add `width: 100%` to .btn-primary, or add a modifier rule `.otp-actions .btn-primary { width: 100%; }`.

title: Tests for buildSocialProviders duplicate implementation instead of testing real code
description:
social-providers.test.ts (lines 16-35) and login-page.test.ts (lines 148-192) both re-implement the buildSocialProviders() algorithm locally instead of importing and testing the real function from better-auth.ts:28-48. If the real implementation changes, these tests still pass because they validate their own copy.

Fix: refactor buildSocialProviders() to accept an optional env parameter (defaulting to process.env), export it, and have both test files import the real function.

title: Fix: dark mode input placeholder uses hardcoded rgb() instead of CSS token (from ePDS-aq2.1)
Auth service should resolve AT Protocol handles in login_hint to emails
description:
When a third-party ATProto app (e.g. sdsls.dev) initiates an OAuth flow, it sends the user's handle (e.g. user.epds1.test.certified.app) as the login_hint parameter. The auth service only recognizes email-shaped login hints (checks for '@' in login-page.ts:121), so handle-based hints are ignored and the user is shown the email form instead of going straight to OTP.

Fix requires:
1. Accept a handle as a login_hint in the auth service
2. Resolve handle → DID (via DNS/HTTP well-known or XRPC)
3. Look up the DID in the local account database to find the associated email
4. Proceed with the OTP flow using that email

Affected files:
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (login_hint processing at line 121)
- packages/shared/src/db.ts (may need a DID-to-email query)
- Possibly packages/auth-service/src/routes/ (handle resolution utility)
close_reason: PAR login-hint flow working: auth service retrieves login_hint from stored PAR request, resolves handles/DIDs to emails via /_internal/account-by-handle, and skips to OTP. Also fixed missing PDS_INTERNAL_URL causing timeouts in Docker.

title: Epic: Add OTP_CHARSET env var for alphanumeric OTP support
description:
## Goal
Add an OTP_CHARSET env var (numeric | alphanumeric) to the auth service so operators can switch OTP codes from digits-only (0-9) to uppercase alphanumeric (A-Z + 0-9, full 36-char set).

## Context
- better-auth's emailOTP plugin has a generateOTP hook that accepts a custom generator function
- better-auth/crypto exports generateRandomString which accepts named alphabet tokens ('A-Z', '0-9', etc.)
- The existing OTP_LENGTH config is already threaded through AuthServiceConfig → createBetterAuth → runBetterAuthMigrations — otpCharset follows the same pattern
- storeOTP: 'hashed' (SHA-256) works with any charset — no schema changes needed
- Default is 'numeric' (backwards compatible)

## Scope
- 5 source files in packages/auth-service/src/
- 2 .env.example documentation files
- 1 test file for new alphanumeric coverage
- No new dependencies, no schema changes, no new files

## Key constraints
- Use ternary spread (...(condition ? { key: val } : {})) not && spread — TypeScript strict mode rejects spreading false
- createBetterAuth takes positional args (emailSender, db, otpLength) — add otpCharset as explicit 4th arg
- recovery.ts renderOtpForm has NO inline JS — only server-rendered HTML attrs and subtitle string
- generateOTP in the migration temp instance is for API consistency only, not functional


## Don't

- Don't add JSDoc — the field name is self-documenting

title: Parse and validate OTP_CHARSET env var in index.ts
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/index.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Parse OTP_CHARSET in the config object (line 138, after otpLength)
```ts
otpLength: parseInt(process.env.OTP_LENGTH || '8', 10),
otpCharset: (process.env.OTP_CHARSET || 'numeric') as 'numeric' | 'alphanumeric',
```

### 2. Add validation block (after the existing otpLength validation at line 148)
```ts
const validCharsets = ['numeric', 'alphanumeric']
if (!validCharsets.includes(config.otpCharset)) {
  throw new Error(
    \`Invalid OTP_CHARSET: must be 'numeric' or 'alphanumeric', got \"${process.env.OTP_CHARSET}\"\`,
  )
}
```

### 3. Thread otpCharset into runBetterAuthMigrations call (line 150-154)
Add config.otpCharset as 4th argument:

```ts
await runBetterAuthMigrations(config.dbLocation, config.hostname, config.otpLength, config.otpCharset)
```

```ts
const betterAuthInstance = createBetterAuth(
  ctx.emailSender,

```

title: Add generateOTP alphanumeric support to better-auth.ts
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Add import at top (after existing imports, ~line 19)
```ts
import { generateRandomString } from 'better-auth/crypto'
```

### 2. Update runBetterAuthMigrations signature (line 76-80)
Add 4th parameter with default:
```ts
export async function runBetterAuthMigrations(
  dbLocation: string,
  authHostname: string,
  otpLength: number,
  otpCharset: 'numeric' | 'alphanumeric' = 'numeric',
): Promise<void>
```

### 3. Add generateOTP to migration temp emailOTP block (~line 88-94)
Add this line inside the emailOTP({...}) options, after storeOTP:
```ts
emailOTP({
  otpLength,
  expiresIn: 600,
  allowedAttempts: 5,
  storeOTP: 'hashed',
  ...(otpCharset === 'alphanumeric'
    ? { generateOTP: () => generateRandomString(otpLength, 'A-Z', '0-9') }
    : {}),
  async sendVerificationOTP() {},
})
```

IMPORTANT: Use ternary (...(cond ? {...} : {})), NOT && spread. TypeScript strict mode rejects spreading false.

Note: generateOTP in the migration instance is for API consistency only — it has no effect on schema.

### 4. Update createBetterAuth signature (line 117-121)
Add 4th parameter with default:
```ts
export function createBetterAuth(
  emailSender: EmailSender,
  db: EpdsDb,
  otpLength: number,
  otpCharset: 'numeric' | 'alphanumeric' = 'numeric',
): any
```

### 5. Add generateOTP to production emailOTP block (~line 157-161)
Same ternary spread pattern as step 3, placed after storeOTP and before the sendVerificationOTP callback:
```ts
...(otpCharset === 'alphanumeric'
  ? { generateOTP: () => generateRandomString(otpLength, 'A-Z', '0-9') }
  : {}),
```

## How generateRandomString works
- Exported from 'better-auth/crypto'
- Signature: generateRandomString<SubA extends 'a-z' | 'A-Z' | '0-9' | '-_'>(length: number, ...alphabets: SubA[]): string
- 'A-Z' expands to ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ, '0-9' expands to 0123456789
- Combined: 36-char alphabet, uses crypto.getRandomValues() internally

## Don't
- Don't use && spread pattern — it fails TypeScript strict mode
- Don't modify index.ts, context.ts, or any route files
- Don't change the sendVerificationOTP callback
- Don't change any other emailOTP options (expiresIn, allowedAttempts, storeOTP)
close_reason: 997fe95 Add generateOTP alphanumeric support to better-auth.ts

title: Update login-page.ts OTP input and JS for charset support
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Add otpCharset to renderLoginPage opts type (~line 207)
After the existing otpLength field:
```ts
otpLength: number
otpCharset: 'numeric' | 'alphanumeric'
```

### 2. Pass otpCharset from the route handler (~line 188)
```ts
otpLength: ctx.config.otpLength,
otpCharset: ctx.config.otpCharset,
```

### 3. Update OTP input HTML attributes (line 324-327)
Current code:
```html
<input type="text" id="code" name="code" required
       maxlength="${opts.otpLength}" pattern="[0-9]{${opts.otpLength}}" inputmode="numeric"
       autocomplete="one-time-code" placeholder="${'0'.repeat(opts.otpLength)}" class="otp-input"
       style="letter-spacing: ${Math.max(2, Math.round(32 / opts.otpLength))}px">
```

Replace with charset-aware version:
- pattern: `[0-9]{${N}}` → `[A-Za-z0-9]{${N}}` when alphanumeric (accept lowercase input, OTP is uppercase but be lenient on input)
- inputmode: `numeric` → `text` when alphanumeric
- Add autocapitalize: `characters` when alphanumeric, `off` when numeric
- placeholder: `'0'.repeat(N)` → `'X'.repeat(N)` when alphanumeric
- Keep autocomplete="one-time-code" unchanged
- Keep the letter-spacing style unchanged

### 4. Inject otpCharset into inline script (~line 363, after var otpLength)
```js
var otpLength = ${opts.otpLength};
var otpCharset = ${JSON.stringify(opts.otpCharset)};
```

### 5. Update subtitle copy in showOtpStep function (~line 370)
Current:
```js
otpSubtitle.textContent = 'We sent ' + article + ' ' + otpLength + '-digit code to ' + masked;
```
Change to:
```js
otpSubtitle.textContent = 'We sent ' + article + ' ' + otpLength + (otpCharset === 'alphanumeric' ? '-character' : '-digit') + ' code to ' + masked;
```

### 6. Update subtitle copy in auto-send then callback (~line 502)
Same change — find the second occurrence of '-digit code to ' in the script and apply the same ternary.

## Don't
- Don't change autocomplete="one-time-code" — keep it for SMS autofill
- Don't change the article computation — it's based on the number, not the word, and works correctly for both
- Don't modify any other route files
- Don't modify better-auth.ts or context.ts

title: Bug: recovery.ts creates auth_flow with clientId: null despite clientId being in scope
description:
## Finding

In `packages/auth-service/src/routes/recovery.ts` lines 114-119, when the recovery POST handler creates a new auth_flow row (because no valid cookie exists), it hardcodes `clientId: null` even though `clientId` is available in scope from `req.body.client_id` (line 71).

```ts
// recovery.ts:111-119
let flowId = req.cookies[AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE] as string | undefined
if (!flowId || !ctx.db.getAuthFlow(flowId)) {
  const { randomBytes } = await import('node:crypto')
  flowId = randomBytes(16).toString('hex')
  ctx.db.createAuthFlow({
    flowId,
    requestUri,
    clientId: null,   // <-- BUG: clientId is in scope but not passed
    expiresAt: Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000,
  })
```

## Impact

When a user arrives at recovery without a valid auth_flow cookie (e.g. cookie expired, new browser session, privacy tool stripped it), the newly created auth_flow row has `clientId: null`. Downstream:

1. `/auth/complete` reads `flow.clientId` to check `hasClientLogin(email, clientId)` — with null clientId it skips the consent check entirely (line 97: `!isNewAccount && clientId && ...`), so consent is never shown for recovery flows even when it should be.
2. `resolveCustomCss` in recovery GET/POST reads clientId from `req.body` / `req.query` (not from the flow), so CSS branding still works on the recovery pages themselves — but the null clientId in the DB means consent and any downstream clientId-dependent logic silently breaks.
3. The existing test at `recovery.test.ts:91-101` explicitly asserts `expect(flow!.clientId).toBeNull()` — it was written to match the buggy behaviour, not to verify correct behaviour.

## Fix

Change line 118 from `clientId: null` to `clientId: clientId ?? null` so the available clientId is stored in the row.

title: Fix: auth.steps.ts missing imports for clearMailpit, sharedBrowser, createAccountViaOAuth (from ePDS-geiz)
description:
Review of ePDS-geiz found: auth.steps.ts uses clearMailpit (lines 496, 525), sharedBrowser (lines 490, 518), and createAccountViaOAuth (line 486) but none of these are imported. The file only imports { waitForEmail, extractOtp } from mailpit.js and has no import for common.steps.js or hooks.js.

Running `npx tsc -p e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json --noEmit` confirms:
  e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts(486,11): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'createAccountViaOAuth'.
  e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts(490,26): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'sharedBrowser'.
  e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts(496,11): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'clearMailpit'.
  e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts(518,26): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'sharedBrowser'.
  e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts(525,11): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'clearMailpit'.

The affected steps are in the '{string} has previously approved the demo client' Given step (consent-screen.feature Scenario 3). At runtime these would throw ReferenceError, causing the consent-screen returning-user scenario to fail.

Fix: add to auth.steps.ts imports:
  import { waitForEmail, extractOtp, clearMailpit } from '../support/mailpit.js'
  import { createAccountViaOAuth } from './common.steps.js'
  import { sharedBrowser } from '../support/hooks.js'

title: Fix: .otp-links justify-content:flex-end misaligns secondary buttons (from ePDS-yzs)
description: Review of ePDS-yzs found that .otp-links uses justify-content:flex-end (line 619) but the ePDS-yzs spec says secondary buttons should be 'side-by-side' (centered or left-aligned under the primary). With flex-end the 'Use Different Email · Resend Code' row is right-aligned, which looks unbalanced under a full-width primary button. The previous commit (ePDS-f9m) changed this to center, but the final ePDS-yzs commit reverted it to flex-end. Should be justify-content:center to match the visual design intent.

title: Tests for sendVerificationOTP duplicate callback logic instead of testing real code
description:
better-auth-otp.test.ts (lines 93-166) re-implements the sendVerificationOTP callback logic from better-auth.ts:164-201 — flowId cookie lookup, isNewUser determination, clientId resolution, and sendOtpCode parameter assembly. The test validates its own copy, not the real callback.

Fix: extract the core logic (cookie → DB lookup → OTP context resolution) into a named exported helper function. The better-auth callback becomes a thin wrapper. Tests import and call the helper directly.

title: Fix: title-panel logo and text layout broken on mobile (from ePDS-az2)
description: On mobile (<768px), .title-panel is display:flex flex-direction:row with the logo and a div containing the SVG title and subtitle. The .client-logo is 48x48px and sits inline with the title SVG (200px wide). On a 320px screen this leaves only 320-48-12(gap)-24(padding*2)=236px for the SVG, but the SVG is 200px so it fits — barely. However, the subtitle paragraph has display:none on mobile (line 497-501) which is correct. The issue is that when no logo is present (logoHtml=''), the title-panel-inner div still has flex-direction:row with gap:12px but only one child (the div with SVG+subtitle). This is fine. But when a logo IS present, the logo sits to the LEFT of the SVG title on mobile, making the header look like a nav bar rather than a branded header. On desktop (>=768px) the logo should be ABOVE the title (margin-bottom:16px is set). Consider flex-direction:column on mobile for title-panel-inner when a logo is present.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI (prettier, eslint, tests)
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on PRs and pushes to main: 1) prettier --check, 2) eslint, 3) pnpm typecheck, 4) pnpm test. Should use pnpm caching for speed. Depends on Prettier and ESLint being set up first.
close_reason: CI workflow created: format:check, lint, typecheck, test. All 4 steps verified passing locally.

title: Fix: random handle retry loop retries all errors, not just collisions — inconsistent with chosen handle path
description:
Review of the handle mode system found: in pds-core/src/index.ts, the random handle retry loop (lines 252-282) catches ALL errors and retries up to 3 times before re-throwing. This is inconsistent with the chosen handle path (lines 220-243) which correctly distinguishes HandleUnavailableError (collision/reserved) from other errors.

For the chosen handle path:
- HandleUnavailableError -> redirect to handle picker (correct)
- Other errors -> 500 immediately (correct)

For the random handle path:
- ALL errors -> retry up to 3 times -> then throw (incorrect)

If the error is NOT a collision (e.g. invite code exhausted, datastore failure, network error), the random path wastes 3 attempts before failing. This delays the error response and may cause confusing log noise (3 'Account creation attempt failed, retrying' warnings for a non-retryable error).

The fix: in the random handle retry loop, only retry on HandleUnavailableError. For other errors, break immediately and re-throw (or return 500 directly).

Evidence: packages/pds-core/src/index.ts lines 252-282 (random loop, catches all errors); lines 220-243 (chosen handle path, distinguishes HandleUnavailableError).

title: Idempotency path in login-page.ts silently drops updated handleMode on duplicate GET
description: Review of 0962a39 found: In login-page.ts, when a duplicate GET /oauth/authorize arrives for an existing request_uri (existingFlow != null), the code reuses the existing flowId and skips createAuthFlow entirely (lines 88-99). This means the epds_handle_mode query param from the duplicate request is silently ignored — the stored handleMode from the FIRST request is used. This is probably fine in practice (the PAR request_uri is tied to a single client call), but it creates a subtle footgun: if a client sends two GETs with different epds_handle_mode values (e.g. first without the param, then with 'random'), the second request's mode is silently dropped and the user gets the picker instead of random assignment. The log message says 'dropping duplicate' but does not log the incoming handleMode, making this invisible in production. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 88-99 and 103-109.

title: Fix: recovery OTP form missing label for code input (from ePDS-aq2.5)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.5 found: The OTP verification form in renderOtpForm() has no <label> element for the code input field (id="code"). The recovery form (renderRecoveryForm) correctly has <label class="field-label" for="email">Backup email address</label>, but the OTP form skips the label entirely.

Evidence:
- Line 232: renderRecoveryForm has <label class="field-label" for="email">Backup email address</label>
- Lines 298-310: renderOtpForm .field div has only .input-container/.input-inner/.input-icon/input — no label
- The login-page.ts OTP step (line 773) has <label class="field-label" for="code">One-time code</label>

Fix: Add <label class="field-label" for="code">Recovery code</label> (or similar) inside the .field div in renderOtpForm(), before the .input-container, matching the pattern in renderRecoveryForm and login-page.ts.

title: CSP fix does not address logo_uri on different origin than client_id (ePDS-0jl still open)
description: The proposed CSP fix adds the client_id origin to img-src via cookie→DB fallback. However, ePDS-0jl (already open) documents that logo_uri may be hosted on a different origin than client_id. For example: - client_id: https://app.example.com/client-metadata.json → adds app.example.com to img-src - logo_uri: https://cdn.example.com/logo.png → still blocked by CSP The proposed fix only resolves the 'client_id absent from query/body' problem. It does not resolve the 'logo_uri on different CDN origin' problem. Both issues must be fixed together or the logo will still be blocked in the CDN case even after the cookie fallback is added. The fix should also extract the origin from logo_uri (resolved from client metadata) and add it to img-src. Since the CSP middleware runs before route handlers and doesn't have access to resolved client metadata, the cleanest solution may be to move img-src construction into the route handlers themselves (where metadata is already resolved) and set it via res.setHeader() before rendering, rather than in global middleware. Evidence: - index.ts line 48-60: only client_id origin is added to img-src - login-page.ts line 244-246: logo_uri is rendered as - ePDS-0jl: existing open issue documenting this exact gap

title: Settings page broken after login
description: After a successful OAuth login, the settings page in the client app is broken. Root cause unknown — needs investigation. Discovered during end-to-end testing of the maearth-demo app.
close_reason: Fixed in maearth-demo app, not an ePDS issue.

title: Fix: missing aria-live region for dynamic error/success messages (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: The #error-msg div has role='alert' (line 802) which is correct for errors. However, when showSuccess() is called (e.g. 'Code resent!'), the element still has role='alert' which is appropriate for assertive announcements. But the element starts with display:none and is shown dynamically — screen readers may not announce content injected into a hidden element that is then shown. The pattern should use aria-live='polite' for success messages and aria-live='assertive' (or role='alert') for errors. Since the same element is reused for both, consider splitting into two separate elements, or dynamically toggling aria-live between 'polite' and 'assertive' in showError()/showSuccess().
close_reason: 7f6a3f8 fix: dynamic role for error/success announcements

title: Epic: E2E test framework — cucumber-js + Playwright against Railway
description:
## Goal
Wire up a working E2E BDD test framework (cucumber-js + Playwright + tsx) that runs the existing passwordless-authentication.feature against live Railway services. Browser tests run headed so the developer can watch.

## Context
- 15 Gherkin .feature files (86 scenarios) already exist in features/
- Three Railway services are live and healthy:
  - PDS core: https://karmas-e2e-pds.up.railway.app
  - Auth service: https://certified-e2e-auth.up.railway.app
  - Demo app: https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app
- No MailHog yet — OTP-dependent steps call this.pending() and skip gracefully
- No Docker-local environment — Railway-first, Docker comes later
- No CI workflow yet — local runs only for now

## Scope
- Framework wiring: cucumber-js config, tsx loader, Playwright browser, tsconfig
- Cucumber World class with page lifecycle and pending() helper
- Step definitions for passwordless-authentication.feature (auth flow up to OTP form)
- Email step stubs that go pending when E2E_MAILHOG_URL is unset
- Headed browser by default (E2E_HEADLESS defaults to false)

## Success
Running 'pnpm test:e2e' opens a browser, navigates to the demo app, clicks sign in, follows the OAuth redirect to the auth service, enters an email, sees the OTP form appear, then the MailHog-dependent steps go pending. No red failures. The feature file is untouched.

## Out of scope
- Docker-local environment (dnsmasq, Caddy internal CA, compose overlay)
- CI/GitHub Actions workflows
- Step definitions for any feature file other than passwordless-authentication.feature
- MailHog deployment or OTP retrieval
- Stagehand integration (plain Playwright for now)
close_reason: f1a4662 superseded: recreating with e2e/ directory layout

title: Install dependencies and configure cucumber-js runner
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- cucumber.mjs (create)

## What to do
Add three devDependencies to root package.json:
- `@cucumber/cucumber` ^11.0.0
- `@playwright/test` ^1.50.0
- `tsx` ^4.0.0

Add two scripts to root package.json:
- `"test:e2e": "node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js"`
- `"test:e2e:ci": "E2E_HEADLESS=true node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js"`

Create cucumber.mjs at root with:
```js
export default {
  paths: ['features/**/*.feature'],
  import: ['features/step-definitions/**/*.ts', 'features/support/**/*.ts'],
  format: ['progress-bar', 'html:reports/e2e.html'],
  publishQuiet: true,
  tags: 'not @manual',
}
```

Key details:
- Use `import:` NOT `require:` — the repo is ESM (`"type": "module"` in package.json)
- Use tsx as the ESM TypeScript loader, NOT ts-node (ts-node/register is CJS-only and will throw ERR_REQUIRE_ESM)
- Do NOT add a `requireModule` field — tsx handles TS via the node --import flag
- The `@playwright/test` package includes both the playwright library AND the expect API with auto-waiting matchers (toBeVisible, toHaveText, etc.). Plain `playwright` does NOT include expect.

Run `pnpm install` after modifying package.json. Then run `npx playwright install chromium` to download the browser binary.

## Don't
- Don't add ts-node as a dependency
- Don't add plain `playwright` (use `@playwright/test` which includes it)
- Don't use `require:` or `requireModule:` in cucumber.mjs
- Don't add a playwright.config.ts — it's not needed (Playwright is used as a library, not a runner)
- Don't modify any existing scripts
close_reason: f1a4662 superseded: recreating with e2e/ directory layout

title: Create tsconfig.e2e.json for features/ TypeScript
description:
## Files
- tsconfig.e2e.json (create)

## What to do
Create tsconfig.e2e.json at the repo root. This is needed because the root tsconfig.json is a composite project-references config that only covers packages/. The features/ directory TypeScript files need their own config.

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "outDir": ".e2e-dist",
    "rootDir": ".",
    "types": ["node"]
  },
  "include": [
    "features/**/*.ts"
  ]
}
```

Then update the test:e2e script in package.json to tell tsx to use this config:
```
"test:e2e": "TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.e2e.json node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js"
"test:e2e:ci": "TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.e2e.json E2E_HEADLESS=true node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js"
```

Key details:
- Use `NodeNext` for both module and moduleResolution (matches repo convention, forward-compatible alias for Node16)
- The `outDir` is `.e2e-dist` — tsx doesn't actually emit files there, but TypeScript needs it set when rootDir is `."
- Do NOT add this tsconfig to the root tsconfig.json references array — it's standalone, not part of the composite build
- Import paths in features/*.ts files should use .js extensions per repo convention

## Don't
- Don't modify tsconfig.json (the root composite config)
- Don't add features/ to any existing tsconfig
- Don't set moduleResolution to 'bundler' — must be NodeNext for consistency

title: Create features/support/env.ts — test environment config
description:
## Files
- features/support/env.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the environment configuration module that all step definitions and hooks import. It reads from environment variables with Railway defaults.

```ts
export const testEnv = {
  pdsUrl: process.env.E2E_PDS_URL ?? 'https://karmas-e2e-pds.up.railway.app',
  authUrl: process.env.E2E_AUTH_URL ?? 'https://certified-e2e-auth.up.railway.app',
  demoUrl: process.env.E2E_DEMO_URL ?? 'https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app',
  mailhogUrl: process.env.E2E_MAILHOG_URL ?? '',
  headless: process.env.E2E_HEADLESS === 'true',
}
```

Key details:
- `headless` defaults to `false` (headed) — only true when E2E_HEADLESS is explicitly 'true'. This is intentional: the developer wants to see the browser.
- `mailhogUrl` defaults to empty string. When empty, MailHog-dependent steps will call `this.pending()` and skip. When set (e.g. 'http://localhost:8025'), those steps execute.
- All URLs should NOT have trailing slashes.
- This file has no side effects — it's a pure config export.
- Use .js extension in any imports from this file (e.g. `import { testEnv } from './env.js'`) per repo convention.

## Don't
- Don't add any Railway secrets or API keys
- Don't add Docker-local defaults (pds.test, auth.pds.test) — Railway-first
- Don't import anything from the monorepo packages (features/ is standalone)

title: Create features/support/world.ts — Cucumber World class with Playwright page
description:
## Files
- features/support/world.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the custom Cucumber World class that holds the Playwright page and provides helpers. This is the central object available as `this` in all step definitions.

```ts
import { World, type IWorldOptions, setWorldConstructor } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import type { BrowserContext, Page } from '@playwright/test'
import { testEnv } from './env.js'

export class EpdsWorld extends World {
  declare context: BrowserContext
  declare page: Page

  constructor(options: IWorldOptions) {
    super(options)
  }

  get env() {
    return testEnv
  }

  /**
   * Call in any step that requires MailHog. If E2E_MAILHOG_URL is not set,
   * marks the step as pending and cucumber-js skips remaining steps in the scenario.
   * When MailHog is available, this is a no-op and the step executes normally.
   */
  skipIfNoMailhog(): void {
    if (!testEnv.mailhogUrl) {
      return this.pending()
    }
  }
}

setWorldConstructor(EpdsWorld)
```

Key details:
- `context` and `page` are set by the Before hook in hooks.ts (not in the constructor). Use `declare` to tell TypeScript they exist without initializing them.
- The browser instance is NOT on the World — it's a module-level variable in hooks.ts. This is because BeforeAll and Before have different `this` contexts in cucumber-js. BeforeAll's `this` is NOT a World instance.
- `this.pending()` is a cucumber-js method that throws the magic string 'pending'. Cucumber catches it and marks the step as pending (yellow in reports), then skips remaining steps in the scenario. It does NOT mark the scenario as failed.
- `setWorldConstructor(EpdsWorld)` must be called at module level so cucumber-js uses our World class.
- Step definitions type `this` as `EpdsWorld`: `Given('...', async function(this: EpdsWorld) { ... })`

## Don't
- Don't store `browser: Browser` on the World class (BeforeAll can't set it — different `this`)
- Don't import Browser from playwright — only BrowserContext and Page are needed on the World
- Don't call `this.skip()` — that method doesn't exist in cucumber-js. Use `this.pending()`
- Don't make skipIfNoMailhog async — pending() is synchronous (it throws)
close_reason: f1a4662 superseded: recreating with e2e/ directory layout

title: Create features/support/hooks.ts — browser lifecycle and screenshot on failure
description:
## Files
- features/support/hooks.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the Cucumber hooks file that manages the Playwright browser lifecycle and captures screenshots on failure.

```ts
import { BeforeAll, AfterAll, Before, After, setDefaultTimeout } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import { chromium, type Browser } from '@playwright/test'
import { mkdir } from 'node:fs/promises'
import type { EpdsWorld } from './world.js'
import { testEnv } from './env.js'

// Railway services have cold-start latency. Default cucumber step timeout
// is 5 seconds which is too short for OAuth redirect chains.
setDefaultTimeout(60_000)

let sharedBrowser: Browser

BeforeAll(async function () {
  await mkdir('reports/screenshots', { recursive: true })
  sharedBrowser = await chromium.launch({ headless: testEnv.headless })
})

Before(async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  this.context = await sharedBrowser.newContext()
  this.page = await this.context.newPage()
  this.page.setDefaultNavigationTimeout(30_000)
  this.page.setDefaultTimeout(15_000)
})

After(async function (this: EpdsWorld, scenario) {
  if (scenario.result?.status === 'FAILED') {
    const safeName = scenario.pickle.name
      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/gi, '-')
      .replace(/^-|-$/g, '')
      .toLowerCase()
      .slice(0, 100)
    await this.page?.screenshot({
      path: `reports/screenshots/${safeName}.png`,
      fullPage: true,
    })
  }
  await this.context?.close() // closes page too
})

AfterAll(async function () {
  await sharedBrowser?.close()
})
```

Key details:
- `sharedBrowser` is a MODULE-LEVEL variable, NOT a World property. BeforeAll's `this` is not a World instance — you cannot set `this.browser` in BeforeAll and read it in Before.
- `setDefaultTimeout(60_000)` — 60 seconds per step. Railway cold starts + OAuth redirect chains can take 10-20 seconds. The default 5s will cause false failures.
- Each scenario gets its own BrowserContext (isolated cookies, storage, auth state). The page is created from the context. Both are closed in After.
- Screenshots are saved on FAILURE only, with sanitized filenames (no special chars, max 100 chars).
- `mkdir('reports/screenshots', { recursive: true })` in BeforeAll ensures the directory exists before any After hook tries to save a screenshot.
- The After hook checks `scenario.result?.status === 'FAILED'` — the status property comes from cucumber-js's ITestCaseHookParameter.

## Don't
- Don't store browser on the World class
- Don't use `scenario.result.status === 'PENDING'` for screenshots — only capture on failure
- Don't set timeout lower than 30 seconds — Railway latency will cause flakes
- Don't close the browser in After (only close context) — browser is shared across scenarios
- Don't use `this.page.close()` — closing the context closes the page automatically
close_reason: f1a4662 superseded: recreating with e2e/ directory layout

title: Create features/step-definitions/common.steps.ts — shared Background steps
description:
## Files
- features/step-definitions/common.steps.ts (create)

## What to do
Implement the Background steps that appear in passwordless-authentication.feature (and most other feature files). These run before every scenario.

Steps to implement:

### 1. `Given the ePDS test environment is running`
Make an HTTP GET to `{testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health` and assert the response status is 200. This confirms the Railway PDS is reachable before the scenario runs. Use the global `fetch` (Node 18+ built-in).

If the health check fails, throw an error with a clear message like 'PDS health check failed: {status} at {url}'. This will fail the scenario immediately rather than letting it run against a dead service.

### 2. `Given a demo OAuth client is registered`
No-op. The Railway demo app is always registered as an OAuth client via its /client-metadata.json endpoint. Just return (empty step body).

### 3. `Given {string} already has a PDS account`
For now, this is a no-op / pending step. Creating accounts requires the full OTP flow which needs MailHog. Call `this.skipIfNoMailhog()` — when MailHog is available later, this step will be implemented to create an account via the demo app's login flow.

```ts
import { Given } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import type { EpdsWorld } from '../support/world.js'
import { testEnv } from '../support/env.js'

Given('the ePDS test environment is running', async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  const res = await fetch(`${testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health`)
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(`PDS health check failed: ${res.status} at ${testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health`)
  }
})

Given('a demo OAuth client is registered', async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  // No-op: Railway demo app is always registered via /client-metadata.json
})

Given('{string} already has a PDS account', async function (this: EpdsWorld, email: string) {
  // Requires creating an account via the full OTP flow (needs MailHog)
  this.skipIfNoMailhog()
})
```

Key details:
- Every step function must use `function(this: EpdsWorld)` syntax, NOT arrow functions. Arrow functions don't bind `this` and cucumber-js won't be able to inject the World.
- Import `testEnv` from `../support/env.js` (with .js extension per repo convention)
- The health check uses global `fetch` — no need to import it (Node 18+)

## Don't
- Don't use arrow functions for step definitions (`() => {}`) — `this` won't work
- Don't import from monorepo packages (@certified-app/*)
- Don't make the health check retry on failure — fail fast so the developer knows the service is down
- Don't implement account creation yet — just call skipIfNoMailhog()

title: Add .gitignore entries and verify full e2e run
description:
## Files
- .gitignore (modify)

## What to do
Add gitignore entries for e2e artifacts, then run the full test suite to verify everything works end-to-end.

### 1. Add to .gitignore:
```
# E2E test artifacts
reports/
.e2e-dist/
```

### 2. Verify the full run:
```bash
# Install deps (should already be done)
pnpm install

# Install browser binary
npx playwright install chromium

# Run the e2e suite
pnpm test:e2e
```

### Expected behavior:
A Chromium browser window opens (headed mode). For each scenario in passwordless-authentication.feature:

**Scenario 1 (New user authenticates):**
- Browser navigates to https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app
- The demo login page loads with an email input
- The email input is visible (step passes)
- Email 'alice@example.com' is entered and form submitted
- Browser follows OAuth redirect chain to auth service
- OTP form appears on auth service (step passes)
- Next step 'an OTP email arrives in the mail trap' → PENDING (no MailHog)
- Remaining steps skipped

**Scenario 2 (Returning user):**
- Background step 'alice already has a PDS account' → PENDING (needs MailHog for account creation)
- Remaining steps skipped

**Scenarios 3-5 (OTP config, wrong code, lockout):**
- Various steps go PENDING (need MailHog or server config changes)

**Scenario 6 (Refresh page):**
- Browser navigates to demo app
- Page refreshes
- Login page renders normally (step passes)
- 'OTP flow still works to completion' → PENDING

### Expected output:
- 0 failures (red)
- Multiple pending steps (yellow)
- Some passing steps (green)
- HTML report generated at reports/e2e.html
- No 'Undefined step' errors

### 3. Also verify:
- `pnpm typecheck` still passes
- `pnpm test` (unit tests) still passes
- `pnpm lint` still passes (or at least no new errors from e2e files)

## Don't
- Don't commit reports/ or screenshots
- Don't modify any .feature files
- Don't change any existing test configuration

title: VALID_HANDLE_MODES constant and HandleMode type defined inside request handler scope
description: Review of 0962a39 found: In login-page.ts lines 50-61, VALID_HANDLE_MODES (a const array) and HandleMode (a type alias) are declared inside the router.get() handler body, meaning they are re-created on every request. Per AGENTS.md conventions, module-level magic values should be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE constants at module scope. Moving these outside the handler avoids repeated allocation and aligns with the codebase style. The HandleMode type should also be exported from a shared location (or at minimum from the route file) so complete.ts can use it for the flow.handleMode comparison instead of comparing against a raw string literal. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 50-61.

title: AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE constant duplicated in 5 files — must be centralized before adding a 6th copy in index.ts
description:
The string literal 'epds_auth_flow' is already defined as AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE in five separate files:
  - packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts:46
  - packages/auth-service/src/routes/recovery.ts:29
  - packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts:28
  - packages/auth-service/src/routes/consent.ts:12
  - packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts:23

The proposed CSP fix adds a 6th copy in index.ts. If the cookie name ever needs to change, all 6 files must be updated in sync — a rename-miss would silently break authentication.

Fix: Export AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE from a single canonical location. The natural home is packages/shared/src/index.ts (already exports DB types, crypto utils, etc.) or a new packages/auth-service/src/constants.ts. All 5 existing files plus the new index.ts usage should import from that single source.

Evidence: grep for AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE across packages/ shows 6 independent const declarations with the same string value.

title: Fix: resend button race condition allows double OTP send on initial auto-send (from ePDS-aq2.1)
Fix shared timingSafeEqual to use SHA-256 hashing instead of length pre-check
description: The timingSafeEqual() helper in packages/shared/src/crypto.ts has a length pre-check that leaks string length via timing. The dummy comparison (comparing a against itself) doesn't fully mask the difference since it runs at a.length, not b.length. Should hash both values to SHA-256 before comparing, matching the approach used in pds-core's verifyInternalSecret(). This affects all callers of the shared helper.

title: Security: user-controlled client_id weakens CSP img-src on every page load (from ePDS-wae.10)
description:
## Problem

Review of ePDS-wae.10 (commit 9fee49a) found a CSP weakening issue in the security headers middleware added in packages/auth-service/src/index.ts (lines 57-67).

The code adds the origin of the `client_id` query/body parameter to the `img-src` CSP directive on every request:

```ts
const clientId = (req.query.client_id as string) || req.body?.client_id
if (clientId && typeof clientId === 'string') {
  try {
    const clientOrigin = new URL(clientId).origin
    if (clientOrigin && clientOrigin !== 'null') {
      imgSrc += ` ${clientOrigin}`
    }
  } catch { /* not a valid URL, keep default */ }
}
```

`client_id` is a user-supplied query parameter. Any visitor can pass `?client_id=https://evil.com` to add `https://evil.com` to the `img-src` directive for their own page load. This:

1. Allows loading tracking pixels from arbitrary origins on the login page
2. Weakens the CSP for that specific request (though only for images)
3. Could be used to probe internal network origins if the auth service is accessible from a network where SSRF is possible

## Evidence

- packages/auth-service/src/index.ts lines 57-67
- The `client_id` parameter is not validated against any allowlist before being used to expand CSP

## Context

The intent is to allow the OAuth client's logo (from `logo_uri` in client metadata) to load. However, the client metadata fetch already happens in `resolveClientMetadata()` in login-page.ts — the actual logo origin could be extracted there and passed to the CSP builder, rather than trusting the raw `client_id` URL.

## Fix

Option A (preferred): Remove the `client_id`-based CSP expansion entirely. Instead, after resolving client metadata in login-page.ts, extract the `logo_uri` origin and set a response header (e.g. `X-Logo-Origin`) that the CSP middleware can read. Or pass the resolved logo origin directly to a per-response CSP setter.

Option B (minimal): Validate that `client_id` is a registered/known client before adding its origin to CSP. Since ATProto client_ids are URLs to metadata documents, this means fetching the metadata first (which login-page.ts already does) and only then allowing the logo_uri origin.

title: Fix: OTP step subtitle empty on initial server-render when login_hint present but otpAlreadySent=false (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: When initialStep='otp' and otpAlreadySent=false (first load with login_hint), the server renders the otp-subtitle paragraph as empty (line 846-850 renders empty string). The JS then fires sendOtp() and on success sets otpSubtitle.textContent = 'We sent an 8-digit code to ' + masked. However, there is a race: if the page renders and the user immediately looks at the OTP field, the subtitle is blank until the fetch completes. More importantly, if JS is disabled or the fetch fails silently, the subtitle stays blank forever. Fix: render the subtitle server-side as 'Sending code to ${masked}...' and let JS update it to 'We sent...' on success.
close_reason: 171adf1 fix: show 'Sending code to...' subtitle during OTP auto-send

title: Test: no coverage for showRandomButton rendering or picker-with-random GET/POST behavior (from ePDS-cg8)

The new showRandomButton logic (line 176) and the conditional button render (line 479) are entirely untested. Per testing-gaps.md, route handlers should be covered via supertest integration tests.
