title: Epic: Resolve CodeRabbit review for PR #10
description: Resolve open CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #10. 2 actionable comments across 2 files — both about replacing inline display style toggles with CSS classes per AGENTS.md rules.

title: Replace inline display toggles with CSS .hidden class
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify)
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/recovery.ts (modify)

## What to do
Replace all inline `style="display:..."` attributes and `element.style.display` JS mutations with CSS class-based visibility, per AGENTS.md rule: "Use CSS classes to control visibility (hidden, active) — avoid inline display style except for dynamic values set at render time."

### login-page.ts changes

**1. Add `.hidden` CSS rule** in the `<style>` block (near the existing `.step-otp` rule):
```css
.hidden { display: none !important; }
```

**2. Replace static inline display attributes in HTML:**

- `<div id="error-msg" class="admonition error" style="display:none;">` → `<div id="error-msg" class="admonition error hidden">`
- `<span class="admonition-icon admonition-icon-success" style="display:none;" ...>` → `<span class="admonition-icon admonition-icon-success hidden" ...>`
- `<div id="social-section"${opts.initialStep === 'otp' ? ' style="display:none;"' : ''}>` → `<div id="social-section"${opts.initialStep === 'otp' ? ' class="hidden"' : ''}>`

**3. Replace JS `style.display` mutations with classList:**

In the `showError()` function:
- `iconError.style.display = ''` → `iconError.classList.remove('hidden')`
- `iconSuccess.style.display = 'none'` → `iconSuccess.classList.add('hidden')`
- `errorEl.style.display = 'flex'` → `errorEl.classList.remove('hidden')`

In the `showSuccess()` function:
- `iconError.style.display = 'none'` → `iconError.classList.add('hidden')`
- `iconSuccess.style.display = ''` → `iconSuccess.classList.remove('hidden')`
- `errorEl.style.display = 'flex'` → `errorEl.classList.remove('hidden')`

In the `clearError()` function:
- `errorEl.style.display = 'none'` → `errorEl.classList.add('hidden')`

In the `showOtpStep()` function:
- `if (socialSection) socialSection.style.display = 'none'` → `if (socialSection) socialSection.classList.add('hidden')`

In the `showEmailStep()` function:
- `if (socialSection) socialSection.style.display = ''` → `if (socialSection) socialSection.classList.remove('hidden')`

**Important:** The `.hidden` class needs `!important` because the admonition normally has `display: flex` — without `!important`, the class-based hide would lose to the more specific `.admonition` rule. Alternatively, increase specificity: `.admonition.hidden { display: none; }` — either approach works.

### recovery.ts changes

**1. Add `.inline-form` CSS rule** in the `getBaseCSS()` function:
```css
.inline-form { display: inline; }
```

**2. Replace inline style on the resend form:**
- `<form method="POST" action="/auth/recover" style="display:inline;">` → `<form method="POST" action="/auth/recover" class="inline-form">`

This appears in the `renderOtpForm()` function.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any route handler logic
- Do NOT change any functionality — only the mechanism for showing/hiding elements
- Do NOT remove the `.step-otp` or `.step-email` class-based visibility (those already follow the pattern)
- Do NOT change the admonition's visible state styling (it should still render as flex when visible)
close_reason: f00a01e Replace inline display toggles with CSS .hidden class

title: HMAC signature mismatch: random-mode callback omits handle field from signed payload
description: Review of 0962a39 found: In complete.ts (step 5a, random mode), signCallback() is called with callbackParams that has no 'handle' field. However, verifyCallback() in pds-core/src/index.ts always passes handle: handleParam (which will be undefined) into the payload, and signCallback() uses params.handle ?? '' as a sentinel. The signed payload in complete.ts will include an empty string for handle (the ?? '' sentinel), and verifyCallback will also use '' for the absent handle param — so the signatures DO match in the happy path. BUT the URL built with new URLSearchParams({ ...callbackParams, ts, sig }) does NOT include a 'handle' key at all, while verifyCallback reads req.query.handle as string | undefined and passes it as handle: handleParam. This is consistent (both use undefined→''). However, the existing-user path (step 5d) also omits handle from callbackParams and works fine. The real risk is that the random-mode path is the ONLY new-account path that does NOT pass handle, so if pds-core ever changes to require handle for new accounts, this will silently break. More critically: the comment says 'let pds-core generate a random handle via generateRandomHandle()' but pds-core only falls into the random-handle branch when chosenHandle is falsy (no handle param). This is correct behavior, but it is not tested end-to-end — there is no test that verifies the random-mode path actually reaches the random-handle branch in pds-core. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts lines 113-123 vs packages/pds-core/src/index.ts lines 88-102 and 244-277.

title: E2E testing: design implementation tasks from docs/design/e2e-testing.md
description:
Read docs/design/e2e-testing.md and break it into detailed implementation beads issues with proper dependencies.

The design doc identifies 6 implementation steps:
1. Fix hardcoded https:// in pds-core and auth-service (add authBaseUrl helper to shared, patch 5 callsites)
2. Add loopback client ID mode to demo (USE_LOOPBACK_CLIENT env var)
3. Add Playwright to monorepo (dev dep, config, e2e/ directory)
4. Write scripts/e2e.sh (start services, wait for health, run Playwright)
5. Write login e2e test (Flow 1: email login with OTP from MailHog)
6. Write Flow 2 e2e test (button-only login)

Each implementation bead should:
- Reference docs/design/e2e-testing.md
- Be self-contained with specific file paths and changes
- Have correct dependencies (step 1 and 2 can be parallel, 3 depends on both, etc.)

This issue is the planning issue — once the impl beads are created, close this one.

title: Update recovery flow to use better-auth OTP and /_internal/account-by-email
description:
The current recovery flow (routes/recovery.ts) allows login via backup email. Update it to:
1. Use better-auth's OTP infrastructure (auth.api.sendVerificationOTP()) for verification instead of custom token service
2. Call /_internal/account-by-email for backup email→DID lookup instead of reading from account_email table
3. Integrate with the auth_flow table so request_uri is preserved through recovery

This is orthogonal to the main login flow but must use the same bridge pattern (better-auth session → /auth/complete → HMAC-signed magic-callback).

Ref: better-auth-migration-plan.md 'Open question: Recovery via backup email'
close_reason: Rewrote recovery.ts to use better-auth OTP: auth.api.sendVerificationOTP() for sending, auth.api.signInEmailOTP() for verification. After successful OTP, redirects to /auth/complete (bridge pattern) with magic_auth_flow cookie set to thread request_uri. Backup email lookup via ctx.db.getDidByBackupEmail() retained (auth-service-owned data). 9 recovery tests added, all 83 tests pass.

title: Design gap: legacy consent mode comment says 'old OTP path still active' but no code path reaches it
description:
## Finding

The JSDoc comment at `consent.ts:21-26` states:

```
 * 2. Legacy mode: reads request_uri/email/client_id from query params directly
 *    (used by the old OTP path which is still active).
```

However, a search of the entire codebase finds no code path that reaches the legacy consent mode. `complete.ts` always redirects to `/auth/consent?flow_id=...` (line 107-110), which triggers the flow_id branch. No route constructs a `/auth/consent?request_uri=...` URL.

The plan correctly identifies this as dead code (change #6) and proposes removing it. This finding confirms the plan's claim is accurate.

## Risk of removal

The legacy branch in consent POST (lines 139-149) does NOT call `ctx.db.deleteAuthFlow()` or `res.clearCookie()` — because in legacy mode there is no flow to clean up. Removing the branch is safe and actually simplifies the cleanup logic.

The legacy hidden fields in `renderConsent` (lines 212-215) are only rendered when `opts.flowId` is undefined. Since all callers now pass a flowId, these fields are never rendered. Safe to remove.

## Action

The comment should be corrected before or as part of the removal. The plan's scope estimate should include updating the JSDoc.

title: Fix: h1 title hardcoded 'Sign in with Certified' — appName not interpolated (from ePDS-aq2.4)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.4 found: The

title in the title panel (login-page.ts line 711) is hardcoded as 'Sign in with Certified' instead of using the dynamic appName variable. Evidence: - Line 711: `

Sign in with Certified

` - Should be: `

Sign in with ${escapeHtml(appName)}

` - The ePDS-aq2.4 spec explicitly requires: 'Replace with:

Sign in with ${escapeHtml(appName)}

' - The JS button text on line 936 also hardcodes 'Sign In with Certified' instead of using the appName JS variable - The appName JS variable (var appName = ${JSON.stringify(appName)}) is never declared in the script block Impact: When a third-party OAuth client provides client_name in their metadata, the title panel and OTP submit button always show 'Certified' instead of the actual app name. The acceptance criteria for ePDS-aq2.4 item 7 ('An

element with class title exists containing Sign in with text') passes trivially but the dynamic substitution is broken. Fix needed: 1. Line 711: change to `

Sign in with ${escapeHtml(appName)}

` 2. Line 788: change to `` 3. Add `var appName = ${JSON.stringify(appName)};` to the JS block (after line 806) 4. Line 936: change to `btn.textContent = 'Sign In with ' + appName;`

title: Fix: recovery link URL is hardcoded placeholder, not the actual request_uri (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: The recovery link at line 879 is: href='/auth/recover?request_uri=${encodeURIComponent(opts.pdsPublicUrl + '/placeholder')}'. The request_uri is hardcoded as pdsPublicUrl + '/placeholder' — it does not pass the actual PAR request_uri from the current OAuth flow. The recovery route presumably needs the real request_uri to resume the flow after recovery. This means clicking 'Recover with backup email' will pass a bogus request_uri to the recovery handler, likely breaking the recovery flow entirely. Fix: pass opts.requestUri (the actual PAR request_uri) to renderLoginPage and use it in the recovery link.
close_reason: 7c39771 fix: pass actual requestUri to recovery link instead of hardcoded placeholder

title: handleMode not validated at type level: AuthFlowRow.handleMode is string|null not HandleMode|null
description: Review of 0962a39 found: The AuthFlowRow interface in db.ts (line 39) types handleMode as 'string | null', not as the HandleMode union type ('random' | 'picker' | 'picker-with-random' | null). This means complete.ts compares flow.handleMode === 'random' against an untyped string, and TypeScript cannot catch a typo like 'Random' or 'RANDOM'. The HandleMode type is currently defined inside the login-page.ts handler scope (not exported), so it cannot be reused in db.ts or complete.ts. The fix is to: (1) export HandleMode from a shared location (e.g. @certified-app/shared types or a dedicated types.ts in auth-service), (2) type AuthFlowRow.handleMode as HandleMode | null, (3) move VALID_HANDLE_MODES to module scope. This would give compile-time safety on all handleMode comparisons. Evidence: packages/shared/src/db.ts line 39; packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 50-61; packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts line 106.

title: Wire email sending into better-auth sendVerificationOTP callback
description:
## Context

Part of Phase 2 of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md`.

## Problem

better-auth's emailOTP plugin needs a `sendVerificationOTP` callback to actually deliver OTP codes. The existing `EmailSender` class in `packages/auth-service/src/email/sender.ts` supports SMTP, SendGrid, AWS SES, and Postmark, plus custom client-branded templates. This infrastructure should be reused.

## Solution

Wire the existing `EmailSender.sendOtpCode()` into the `sendVerificationOTP` callback in `packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts`.

### Client branding challenge

The current template system needs a `client_id` to resolve branding (logo, colors, email template). During a better-auth emailOTP flow, the `sendVerificationOTP` callback does not have access to the OAuth client context.

Solution: read the `magic_auth_flow` cookie from the request context to recover the `flow_id`, look up the `auth_flow` row to get the `client_id`, and pass it to `emailSender.sendOtpCode()`.

If the request does not have a `magic_auth_flow` cookie (e.g., account settings login), use the default PDS email template.

## Files to modify

- `packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts` — wire sendVerificationOTP callback

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] OTP emails delivered via existing EmailSender infrastructure
- [ ] Client branding applied when client_id available from auth_flow
- [ ] Default template used when no client_id context
- [ ] All four email providers (SMTP, SendGrid, SES, Postmark) still work
- [ ] Dev mode (no provider configured) still logs to console
close_reason: Wired existing EmailSender into sendVerificationOTP callback with client branding support. createBetterAuth() now accepts MagicPdsDb; callback reads magic_auth_flow cookie via ctx.getCookie(), looks up auth_flow to get client_id, passes to emailSender.sendOtpCode(). Falls back to default template when no flow context. 6 tests added. 81 tests pass.

title: Bug: recovery POST /auth/recover/verify fails silently when cookie is missing — no auth_flow created
description:
## Finding

The recovery POST handler at `recovery.ts:68` creates an auth_flow row (if missing) when a valid backup email DID is found. However, the verify handler at `recovery.ts:172` (`POST /auth/recover/verify`) does NOT create or check for an auth_flow — it simply redirects to `/auth/complete` after OTP verification.

```ts
// recovery.ts:212
res.redirect(303, '/auth/complete')
```

`/auth/complete` immediately checks for the `epds_auth_flow` cookie (line 39-46 of complete.ts) and returns 400 if absent. So if the cookie was lost between the POST /auth/recover step and the POST /auth/recover/verify step (e.g. cookie expired in the 10-minute window, or was stripped), the user successfully verifies their OTP but then hits a 400 error at /auth/complete with no recovery path.

## Impact

The user has proven their identity via backup email OTP but cannot complete the auth flow. The error message ('Authentication session expired. Please try again.') gives no guidance on what to do — the user would need to restart from the login page.

## Relationship to the refactoring plan

The plan (change #2) proposes that POST handlers read from cookie → DB instead of req.body. This would make the verify handler also check for the cookie, but it does not address what happens when the cookie is genuinely absent at verify time. The plan should specify: if the cookie is missing at verify time, should the handler (a) error with a clear message directing the user back to the login page, or (b) attempt to reconstruct the flow from the requestUri that was previously threaded through hidden fields?

Under the new model (no hidden fields), option (b) is impossible. The plan must explicitly handle this failure mode.

title: Fix: PDS error treated as isNewUser=true in sendVerificationOTP (from ePDS-01p.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-01p.1 found: in better-auth.ts sendVerificationOTP callback (line 172), the isNewUser determination uses:
  const isNewUser = !result?.did

When getDidByEmail returns null (PDS error / timeout), result?.did is undefined, so !undefined === true. This means a PDS outage causes all OTP emails to be sent with isNewUser=true, showing the wrong email template (sign-up template instead of sign-in template) to existing users.

The new return type semantics from ePDS-01p.1 distinguish null (PDS error) from { did: null } (not found), but this call site does not use that distinction — it collapses both error and not-found into isNewUser=true.

Fix: handle the null (PDS error) case explicitly. Options:
  1. Default to isNewUser=false on PDS error (safer — existing users get the right template)
  2. Log a warning and proceed with isNewUser=false
  3. Fail the OTP send on PDS error (too disruptive)

Recommended fix:
  const isNewUser = result === null ? false : result.did === null

title: Passwordless accounts missing account row causes device binding FK failure
description:
When creating a new account via magic-callback, the PDS oauth-store's createAccount() skips registerAccount() when password is undefined (condition: email && passwordScrypt). This leaves the account table empty while actor has a row. The account_device table has a FK on account.did (not actor.did), so upsertDeviceAccount() fails with SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY.

Root cause: packages/pds-core/src/index.ts passes password: undefined to provider.accountManager.createAccount(). The PDS oauth-store.js line ~168 gates registerAccount() on both email AND passwordScrypt being truthy.

Fix applied: after createAccount() returns, manually insert a minimal account row (did, email, passwordScrypt: null) via pds.ctx.accountManager.db before calling upsertDeviceAccount(). This satisfies the FK while keeping the account passwordless.

Also discovered: account_device FK references account.did (not actor.did or device.id separately verified). WAL timing was a red herring — same DB connection is used, the row was simply never written.
notes: Additional finding: account.passwordScrypt has a NOT NULL constraint in the schema, so inserting null fails. Used sentinel value 'passwordless' instead — not a valid scrypt hash so password auth will always fail for these accounts.
close_reason: Fixed by passing a random 256-bit password to createAccount() so registerAccount() runs and the account row is created properly.

title: Epic: Restyle login page to match Bluesky OAuth UI (vanilla, no custom branding)
description:
## Goal
Restyle the auth-service login page (GET /oauth/authorize) to match the Bluesky `@atproto/oauth-provider-ui` design. This is a clean baseline — no Certified-specific branding, no GainForest logos, no custom SVGs. Just the standard atproto OAuth look.

## What success looks like
- Split-panel layout: left panel with title/subtitle (bg-slate-100 light, bg-slate-800 dark), right panel with form
- CSS custom property token system matching atproto's (--hue-primary, --color-contrast-*, --color-primary-*)
- Dark mode support via prefers-color-scheme with inverted contrast scale
- Input components styled like atproto's InputContainer (rounded-lg, bg-gray-100, focus ring)
- Primary button with --color-primary background
- Admonition-style error/success messages
- Mobile responsive: stacks vertically on < 768px
- No hardcoded logos, no Certified text, no custom SVGs — just generic 'Sign in' with client_name
- All existing functionality preserved: email OTP flow, social login buttons, recovery link, login_hint auto-send

## Scope
- Only login-page.ts renderLoginPage() function
- CSS + HTML template only, no route logic changes
- No new dependencies, no Tailwind — pure inline CSS in template literal

## Context
Current branch (bsky-signin-page) has the old basic centered-card design (513 lines). The ui-design branch has a Certified-branded version. This epic creates the generic atproto-style baseline that can later be themed per-tenant.

The route handler logic (lines 1-193) and the `renderError()` function (lines 507-513) stay UNCHANGED. The client-side JavaScript logic stays UNCHANGED in behavior. ### Reference The ui-design branch already has this implementation at 1083 lines. Use it as the primary reference. The key difference from the current branch (513 lines, basic centered card) is the CSS token system, split-panel layout, and atproto-style input components. You can see the ui-design branch version with: `git show ui-design:packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts` ### CSS Token System (in `

title: Fix recovery link to pass actual PAR request_uri instead of pdsPublicUrl
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify)

## What to do
The recovery link passes `opts.pdsPublicUrl` as the `request_uri` query parameter, but it should pass the actual PAR `request_uri` (e.g. `urn:ietf:params:oauth:request_uri:...`). This is needed so the recovery flow can resume the correct OAuth session.

### Changes needed:

1. **Add `requestUri` to the `renderLoginPage` opts type** (line ~196-206):
   Add `requestUri: string` to the opts interface.

2. **Pass `requestUri` from the route handler** (line ~188):
   The route handler already has `requestUri` in scope (from `req.query.request_uri`). Add it to the `renderLoginPage()` call:
   ```ts
   res.type("html").send(
     renderLoginPage({
       flowId,
       clientId: clientId ?? "",
       clientName,
       branding: clientMeta,
       loginHint: emailHint,
       initialStep,
       otpAlreadySent,
       csrfToken: res.locals.csrfToken,
       authBasePath: "/api/auth",
       pdsPublicUrl: ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl,
       requestUri,  // <-- ADD THIS
     }),
   );
   ```
   Note: `requestUri` is guaranteed to be a string at this point because the route handler returns early with an error if it is missing (line ~51).

3. **Update the recovery link** (line ~827):
   Change from:
   ```
   /auth/recover?request_uri=${encodeURIComponent(opts.pdsPublicUrl)}
   ```
   To:
   ```
   /auth/recover?request_uri=${encodeURIComponent(opts.requestUri)}
   ```

### Reference
See the ui-design branch for the correct implementation: `git show ui-design:packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts | grep -n "requestUri"`

## Don't
- Do NOT change any CSS or HTML layout
- Do NOT remove pdsPublicUrl from opts (it may be used elsewhere)
- Do NOT change any other route handlers or files
close_reason: 863d9be fix: pass actual PAR requestUri to recovery link instead of pdsPublicUrl

title: Fix dark mode placeholder to use CSS token instead of hardcoded gray
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify)

## What to do
The dark mode `::placeholder` CSS rule uses a hardcoded `rgb(107 114 128)` instead of the CSS token system. This breaks the purple-tinted contrast scale.

Find the dark mode media query section that overrides placeholder colors (around line 543-545). It looks like:
```css
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  .input-field::placeholder { color: rgb(107 114 128); }
}
```

**Remove this entire dark mode placeholder override.** The light-mode rule already uses `var(--color-text-light)` which automatically inverts via the contrast scale in dark mode — no separate override is needed.

If there is a light-mode placeholder rule like:
```css
.input-field::placeholder { color: var(--color-text-light); }
```
Keep that — it works correctly in both modes because `--color-text-light` maps to `--color-contrast-700` which inverts in dark mode.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any HTML structure or JavaScript
- Do NOT change any other CSS rules
- Do NOT change any other files
close_reason: 863d9be Remove hardcoded dark mode placeholder color, use CSS token

title: Replace static image assets with text and client metadata logo
description:
## Files - packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify) ## What to do Remove all references to hardcoded static image files and replace them with plain text and data-driven logo, matching the Bluesky `@atproto/oauth-provider-ui` `LayoutTitlePage` pattern. The local Bluesky reference is at `/home/kzoeps/Projects/atproto/packages/oauth/oauth-provider-ui/src/components/layouts/layout-title-page.tsx`. Key pattern: logo is optional and comes from data (`customizationData.logo`), title is a plain `

` with text. ### Changes needed: **1. Logo — use `b.logo_uri` from client metadata (optional)** Currently (line ~211): ```ts const logoUrl = '/static/gainforest-logo.png' const logoHtml = `` ``` Change to: ```ts const logoHtml = b.logo_uri ? `` : '' ``` The logo is only shown if the OAuth client metadata provides `logo_uri`. No logo by default. This matches the Bluesky pattern where `logo` is optional from `customizationData`. **2. Title — replace SVG with plain `

` text** Currently (line ~745): ```html ``` Replace with: ```html

Sign in with ${escapeHtml(appName)}

``` Where `appName` = `b.client_name || opts.clientName || 'Certified'` (already defined). Style the `

` to match the Bluesky layout: ```css .title { color: var(--color-primary); font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; margin: 0; } @media (min-width: 768px) { .title { font-size: 24px; margin: 16px 0; } } @media (min-width: 1024px) { .title { font-size: 48px; } } ``` **3. OTP submit button — replace SVG icon with plain text** Currently (line ~823): ```html ``` Replace with: ```html ``` **4. JavaScript button text updates — remove SVG references** Currently the JS updates button innerHTML with SVG img tags (lines ~966, ~971). Change to plain text: - Loading state: `btn.textContent = 'Verifying...';` - Reset state: `btn.textContent = 'Sign In with ' + appName;` Note: `appName` needs to be available in the JS scope. Add it as a JSON-stringified variable at the top of the script block: ```js var appName = ${JSON.stringify(appName)}; ``` Then use: `btn.textContent = 'Sign In with ' + appName;` **5. Remove unused CSS** Remove any CSS rules for `.title-svg`, `.btn-icon` that are no longer needed since those elements are gone. **6. Remove the logoUrl variable** The `const logoUrl = '/static/gainforest-logo.png'` line should be removed entirely. ## Don't - Do NOT change the route handler (createLoginPageRouter) - Do NOT change the renderError function - Do NOT change the renderLoginPage function signature - Do NOT remove the subtitle text "Sign in for the interop using Certified" - Do NOT change any other files - Do NOT change the CSS token system or layout structure - Do NOT change the OTP flow logic or social login logic

title: Fix: brandColor comparison uses hardcoded default '#8338ec' instead of constant (from ePDS-wae.10)
description: In renderLoginPage() at line 241, the brand color override is only applied when brandColor !== '#8338ec'. This hardcoded string is the default value. If the default is ever changed (e.g. to match a different atproto version), this comparison will silently break — the override will always be applied even when using the default. Fix: extract the default as a named constant (e.g. const DEFAULT_BRAND_COLOR = '#8338ec') and use it in both the fallback assignment (line 219) and the comparison (line 241).

title: Fix title-panel inner content alignment: justify-items:end on .title-panel, justify-items:start on .title-panel-inner at 768px breakpoint
description: The previous worker changed justify-items on .title-panel from end to start, which moved the entire content block to the left side of the panel. The fix: revert .title-panel desktop to justify-items:end and text-align:right (positions block on right side of panel), and set .title-panel-inner to justify-items:start and text-align:left (left-aligns items within the block relative to each other). Only the @media (min-width: 768px) block in login-page.ts needs changing.
close_reason: 94cff12 fix: title-panel inner content alignment at 768px breakpoint

title: Test: add coverage for ping non-OK response in GET /auth/choose-handle (from ePDS-wh1.6)
description:
Review of ePDS-wh1.6 found: the new ping response check (pingRes.ok guard) in the GET /auth/choose-handle handler has no test coverage. The choose-handle.test.ts only tests HANDLE_REGEX and RESERVED_HANDLES constants — there are no integration tests for the GET handler at all.

The new code path (choose-handle.ts lines 168-178):
```ts
if (!pingRes.ok) {
  logger.warn({ status: pingRes.status, requestUri: result.flow.requestUri }, ...)
  res.status(400).type('html').send(renderError('Session expired, please start over'))
  return
}
```

This early-return path is untested. A regression here would silently render the page even when the PAR request_uri has expired, causing the user to get a confusing error only after submitting the form.

Add a test (or tests) to packages/auth-service/src/__tests__/choose-handle.test.ts (or a new integration test file) that:
1. Mocks the ping-request fetch to return a non-OK response (e.g. 404)
2. Asserts the GET handler returns 400 with an error page
3. Asserts the page is NOT rendered (renderChooseHandlePage not called)

File to modify: packages/auth-service/src/__tests__/choose-handle.test.ts (or add a new integration test file for the GET handler).

title: Fix: .btn-primary missing hover state (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of the login page restyle found that .btn-primary has no :hover CSS rule. The button transitions with 'transition: all 0.3s ease-in-out' but there is nothing to transition to on hover — the button gives no visual feedback when hovered. The atproto reference UI uses a darkened/lightened primary color on hover. Fix: add .btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(0.9); } or equivalent. This also affects keyboard focus — .btn-primary:focus-visible uses outline:2px solid var(--color-primary) which is invisible when the button background IS the primary color. The outline-offset:2px helps but the outline color should be a contrasting color (e.g. white or a darker shade).
close_reason: 1986f53 fix: add .btn-primary:hover:not(:disabled) opacity rule

title: Fix: account-settings env var inconsistency — pdsUrl falls back to pdsPublicUrl unlike other routes (from ePDS-01p.3)
description: Review of ePDS-01p.3 found: In account-settings.ts line 90, pdsUrl is set as 'process.env.PDS_INTERNAL_URL || ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl'. This silently falls back to the public URL if PDS_INTERNAL_URL is not set. In contrast, complete.ts (line 40) and choose-handle.ts (line 42) throw an error at startup if PDS_INTERNAL_URL is missing: 'throw new Error("PDS_INTERNAL_URL and EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET must be set")'. The AGENTS.md docs warn that using the public URL for internal calls fails in Docker (no hairpin NAT). The account-settings fallback means that in a misconfigured Docker deployment, the requireBetterAuth middleware will silently use the public URL, causing getDidByEmail to fail (returning null) on every request, and every authenticated user will see a 503 page. Fix: either throw at startup like the other routes, or at minimum log a warning when falling back to pdsPublicUrl.

title: Eliminate account_email mirror table; use accountManager directly in pds-core
description:
## Context

Part of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md` (design decisions 5–6).

## Problem

pds-core currently opens the auth-service's `magic-pds.sqlite` to look up email→DID via an `account_email` mirror table, with a three-step fallback chain:
1. `magicDb.getDidByEmail(email)` — reads `account_email` in magic-pds.sqlite
2. `magicDb.getDidByBackupEmail(email)` — reads `backup_email` in magic-pds.sqlite
3. `magicDb.getDidFromPdsAccount(email)` — opens `account.sqlite` read-only as a fallback

This is redundant because the upstream PDS already stores email→DID in `account.sqlite`, and `pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccountByEmail()` is a public method that queries it directly.

## Solution

1. In pds-core's `/oauth/magic-callback` handler, replace the `magicDb.getDidByEmail()` + `magicDb.getDidFromPdsAccount()` fallback chain with a single call to `pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccountByEmail(email)`.

2. Keep the backup email lookup — but have it call `/_internal/account-by-email` or the auth-service's own `backup_email` table via HTTP (backup emails are auth-service-owned data with no PDS equivalent).

3. Remove pds-core's `MagicPdsDb` instantiation entirely — pds-core no longer opens the auth-service database.

4. Delete `getDidByEmail`, `getDidFromPdsAccount`, `setAccountEmail` from `packages/shared/src/db.ts`.

5. Drop the `account_email` table from the auth-service SQLite file.

## Files to modify

- `packages/pds-core/src/index.ts` — replace magicDb calls with accountManager; remove MagicPdsDb import and instantiation
- `packages/shared/src/db.ts` — remove getDidByEmail, getDidFromPdsAccount, setAccountEmail, and account_email table creation/migration

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] pds-core no longer imports or instantiates MagicPdsDb
- [ ] pds-core no longer opens magic-pds.sqlite
- [ ] Magic callback uses pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccountByEmail() for email→DID lookup
- [ ] account_email table dropped from auth-service SQLite schema
- [ ] getDidByEmail, getDidFromPdsAccount, setAccountEmail removed from shared/db.ts
- [ ] New account creation still writes to account.sqlite via createAccount (unchanged)
- [ ] Login flow works end-to-end
close_reason: Removed account_email mirror table from shared/db.ts (v5 migration drops it), removed getDidByEmail/getDidFromPdsAccount/setAccountEmail/getEmailByDid methods. Updated pds-core to use pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccountByEmail() directly, removed MagicPdsDb instantiation. Updated auth-service verify-code and account-login to query /_internal/account-by-email. All 63 tests pass.

title: CSP cookie-based img-src allows attacker-controlled origin via cookie injection (img-src scope)
description:
The proposed fix reads flowId from req.cookies['epds_auth_flow'] and uses it to look up a clientId from the DB, then adds that clientId's origin to img-src. The cookie is httpOnly (not readable by JS) but is NOT signed or encrypted — it is a plain random hex string.

Attack scenario: An attacker who can inject a cookie (e.g., via a subdomain that shares the cookie domain, or via a cookie-injection vulnerability) could:
  1. Create a legitimate auth flow with a crafted client_id pointing to attacker.example.com
  2. Set the epds_auth_flow cookie to that flow's ID on the victim's browser
  3. When the victim visits any auth page, the CSP middleware adds attacker.example.com to img-src
  4. The attacker can load tracking pixels or exfiltrate the page URL via image requests from attacker.example.com

This is a low-severity issue because:
  - img-src does not enable script execution
  - The attacker needs cookie injection capability first
  - The existing client_id query param approach has the same vulnerability (attacker can craft a URL with any client_id)

However, the fix should note this trust model explicitly. The clientId stored in auth_flow comes from the original OAuth authorization request — it is not user-controlled input in the normal flow. The risk is acceptable given the existing trust model, but should be documented.

Fix: Add a comment in the CSP middleware explaining why reading clientId from the DB is safe (the value was validated at flow creation time from the OAuth request_uri, not from user input). Optionally, validate that the clientId is an https:// URL before extracting its origin.

title: Fix: btn-primary focus-visible outline invisible on primary-color background (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: The .btn-primary:focus-visible rule sets outline:2px solid var(--color-primary) with outline-offset:2px. When the button background is var(--color-primary) (e.g. #8338ec), the outline is the same color as the button — effectively invisible. WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast) requires a 3:1 contrast ratio for focus indicators. Fix: use a contrasting color for the focus outline, e.g. outline:2px solid var(--color-primary-contrast) or a dark ring. A double-ring approach (box-shadow) like the input-container uses would be more visible: box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--color-primary), 0 0 0 4px var(--color-contrast-0).
close_reason: 1986f53 fix: change focus-visible outline to var(--color-contrast-1000)

title: Security: empty EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET disables internal endpoint auth entirely
description: Review of ePDS-01p.3 found: verifyInternalSecret() in pds-core/src/index.ts line 437 returns false when EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET is unset — which is correct. However, account-settings.ts line 96 reads the secret as: `const internalSecret = process.env.EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET ?? ''`. When the env var is unset, internalSecret is the empty string ''. getDidByEmail then sends x-internal-secret: '' in the request. verifyInternalSecret rejects this (because !secret is true for empty string), returning 401. getDidByEmail treats 401 as non-2xx and returns outer null. requireBetterAuth then returns 503 to every authenticated user — effectively a self-inflicted DoS on the /account page. The real risk is the inverse: if an operator sets EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET='' explicitly (empty string), verifyInternalSecret returns false (correct), but the auth service sends '' as the secret. This is consistent. The gap is that there is NO startup validation that EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET is set and non-empty. A misconfigured deployment silently degrades to 503 on every /account request rather than failing loudly at boot. Additionally, the .env.example shows EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET= (empty) as the default, meaning a developer who copies .env.example without filling in secrets gets a broken /account page with no clear error message. Fix: add startup validation in auth-service index.ts that logs a fatal error and exits if EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET is unset or empty. Also add the same check in pds-core. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/account-settings.ts:96, packages/pds-core/src/index.ts:437, .env.example:55.

title: Install better-auth and mount alongside existing code
description:
## Context

This is Phase 1 of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md` for the full plan.

## Goal

Install better-auth as a dependency, configure it with emailOTP plugin, and mount it on the auth-service Express app alongside the existing routes. No existing behavior changes — this is a foundation-only step.

## Implementation

### 1.1 Install dependencies

```bash
pnpm add better-auth better-sqlite3
# better-sqlite3 is already a dependency — verify version compatibility
```

### 1.2 Create better-auth configuration

Create `packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts` with:
- `betterAuth()` instance configured with:
  - `database`: points to `process.env.DB_LOCATION ?? "./data/magic-pds.sqlite"` (shared auth-service SQLite)
  - `baseURL`: `https://${process.env.AUTH_HOSTNAME}`
  - `basePath`: `/api/auth` (namespaced to avoid route conflicts)
  - `emailOTP` plugin with `otpLength: 8`, `expiresIn: 600`, `allowedAttempts: 5`, `storeOTP: "hashed"`, and `sendVerificationOTP` callback wired to existing `EmailSender`
  - `socialProviders` built dynamically from env vars — `buildSocialProviders()` checks for `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`+`GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID`+`GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`, etc. Only providers with both vars set are included.
  - `session.expiresIn` from `SESSION_EXPIRES_IN` env var (default 7 days)
  - `session.updateAge` from `SESSION_UPDATE_AGE` env var (default 1 day)
- Export `socialProviders` object so login page can conditionally render buttons

### 1.3 Run better-auth migrations

```bash
pnpm dlx @better-auth/cli migrate
```

Creates `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables in the auth-service SQLite file alongside existing custom tables.

### 1.4 Mount on Express

In `packages/auth-service/src/index.ts`, mount before `express.json()`:

```typescript
import { toNodeHandler } from "better-auth/node";
import { auth } from "./better-auth";
app.all("/api/auth/*", toNodeHandler(auth));
```

Existing routes continue to work unchanged.

## Files to create

- `packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts`

## Files to modify

- `packages/auth-service/src/index.ts` — mount better-auth handler
- `packages/auth-service/package.json` — add better-auth dependency
- `.env.example` — add SESSION_EXPIRES_IN, SESSION_UPDATE_AGE, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] better-auth tables created in auth-service SQLite without breaking existing tables
- [ ] `/api/auth/*` endpoints respond (better-auth health check)
- [ ] Existing OTP login flow still works unchanged
- [ ] No behavior change for users
close_reason: Installed better-auth 1.4.18 and better-sqlite3 as auth-service dependencies. Created packages/auth-service/src/better-auth.ts with betterAuth() configured with emailOTP plugin (otpLength:8, expiresIn:600, allowedAttempts:5, storeOTP:hashed) wired to existing EmailSender, dynamic social providers from env vars, and session lifetime from env. Mounted at /api/auth/* in index.ts before express.json(). Also converted auth-service to type:module (ESM) to support better-auth which is ESM-only. Fixed SMTPTransport type import in email/sender.ts. Added MAGIC_INTERNAL_SECRET, SESSION_EXPIRES_IN, SESSION_UPDATE_AGE, GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET to .env.example. All 65 tests pass.

title: Fix: pds-core renderError missing escapeHtml (from ePDS-wh1.6)
description: Review of ePDS-wh1.6 found: the renderError() function added to packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (line 632-639) does NOT call escapeHtml() on the message parameter, unlike every other renderError() in the codebase (choose-handle.ts:567, login-page.ts:511, recovery.ts both use escapeHtml). AGENTS.md mandates: 'Always escape user input with escapeHtml() from @certified-app/shared'. The current call site passes a hardcoded string literal so there is no immediate XSS, but the function signature accepts any string and future callers could pass user-controlled data (e.g. an error message derived from a thrown exception). Fix: import escapeHtml from @certified-app/shared and use it in the template literal, matching the pattern in all other route files. Evidence: git show faeca5f -- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts shows the added renderError at line 629+ without escapeHtml; compare with choose-handle.ts line 567 which uses escapeHtml(message).
close_reason: a3f5444 fix: escape HTML in pds-core renderError

title: Fix: automatic-account-creation Scenario 2 depends on world state from Scenario 1 across scenario boundary (from ePDS-geiz)
description:
Review of ePDS-geiz found: automatic-account-creation.feature Scenario 2 ('Auto-created handle is a random subdomain') uses:
  Given "newuser@example.com" just had an account auto-created

The step definition in pds.steps.ts (line 113) checks:
  if (!this.userDid) { return 'pending' }

This relies on world.userDid being set from Scenario 1 ('First-time user gets an auto-created PDS account'). However, Cucumber creates a fresh EpdsWorld instance for each scenario (the Before hook in hooks.ts creates a new context). world.userDid will always be undefined at the start of Scenario 2, so this step will always return 'pending'.

The step comment says 'No-op if world.userDid is already set (account was just created in the previous scenario)' — but this is incorrect: world state does NOT persist across scenarios in Cucumber.

This means Scenario 2 will always be skipped (pending), even when Mailpit is configured and Scenario 1 runs successfully.

Fix: Scenario 2 should create its own account (call createAccountViaOAuth) rather than relying on cross-scenario state. The step should be:
  Given "newuser@example.com" just had an account auto-created
  -> calls createAccountViaOAuth and sets world.userHandle, then proceeds
close_reason: d739cec fix: Scenario 2 creates its own account instead of relying on cross-scenario world state

title: Enable social providers (Google, GitHub) via env-var config
description:
Phase 4 of the migration plan. Since buildSocialProviders() already auto-detects from env vars and the login page conditionally renders buttons:

1. Document the required env vars (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET) in .env.example
2. Test the full flow: social login → better-auth session → /auth/complete bridge → HMAC-signed magic-callback
3. Verify account linking: same email from social provider links to existing better-auth user
4. Handle email mismatch risk: display warning if social provider email differs from known PDS account

No changes to pds-core or the bridge route needed — the bridge always reads email from the better-auth session regardless of how the user authenticated.

Ref: better-auth-migration-plan.md Phase 4
close_reason: Documented social provider env vars in .env.example with flow description, account linking behavior, email mismatch warning, and links to OAuth credential pages. Social provider buttons already rendered in login-page.ts (atproto-sbw) and auto-detection already in better-auth.ts (atproto-bvg). Added 11 social provider tests. 94 tests total pass.

title: picker-with-random mode stored in DB but never acted upon in complete.ts or choose-handle.ts
description: Review of 0962a39 found: The 'picker-with-random' handle mode is accepted and stored in the DB (login-page.ts line 53, db.ts migration v8), but neither complete.ts nor choose-handle.ts reads or acts on this value. In complete.ts, the only branch that checks handleMode is 'random' (line 106); all other values (including 'picker-with-random') fall through to the same redirect to /auth/choose-handle. In choose-handle.ts, flow.handleMode is never read at all. This means 'picker-with-random' is a dead mode — it behaves identically to 'picker' and 'null'. Either the choose-handle page should render a 'Use random handle instead' option when handleMode is 'picker-with-random', or the mode should be removed from VALID_HANDLE_MODES until it is implemented. Shipping an accepted-but-unimplemented mode creates confusion and a false API contract. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts lines 105-143; packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (no reference to handleMode); packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts line 53.

title: Migrate account settings portal to better-auth sessions
description:
## Context

Phase 3 of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md` Phase 3.

## Problem

The `/account/*` routes on the auth-service currently use a custom `account_session` table and `magic_account_session` cookie for session management. This should use better-auth's built-in session system instead.

## Solution

1. Replace `requireAuth(ctx)` calls with a wrapper that calls `auth.api.getSession({ headers })` to validate the better-auth session.

2. The "Account Settings Login" flow becomes: render login page → user authenticates via better-auth → session established → redirect to auth-service `/account`.

3. Session revocation routes delegate to better-auth:
   - `POST /account/session/revoke` → `auth.api.revokeSession()`
   - `POST /account/sessions/revoke-all` → `auth.api.revokeSessions()`

4. These routes stay custom (they call PDS admin APIs):
   - `POST /account/handle` — calls `com.atproto.admin.updateAccountHandle`
   - `POST /account/delete` — calls `com.atproto.admin.deleteAccount`
   - `POST /account/backup-email/*` — custom backup_email table + verification

5. Update `GET /metrics` to query better-auth's tables (user count, session count) alongside existing custom tables.

### Delete on completion

- `packages/auth-service/src/middleware/account-auth.ts`
- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/account-login.ts`
- `packages/auth-service/src/middleware/session.ts`
- Drop `account_session` table from auth-service SQLite

## Files to modify

- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/account-settings.ts` — use better-auth session
- `packages/auth-service/src/index.ts` — remove old account-auth middleware

## Files to delete

- `packages/auth-service/src/middleware/account-auth.ts`
- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/account-login.ts`
- `packages/auth-service/src/middleware/session.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Account settings pages use better-auth session
- [ ] Old account_session table dropped
- [ ] Session revocation works via better-auth
- [ ] Handle change, account deletion, backup email management still work
- [ ] Metrics endpoint queries better-auth tables
close_reason: Migrated /account/* routes to better-auth session management. Replaced custom account_session table + magic_account_session cookie with better-auth APIs. account-login now uses email OTP via better-auth. account-settings uses auth.api.getSession/listSessions/revokeSession/signOut. v7 migration drops account_session table. Deleted account-auth.ts and session.ts middleware. All 63 tests pass.

title: Fix: social login callbackURL hardcoded to /auth/complete, not flow-aware (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: The social login buttons link to /api/auth/sign-in/social?provider=google&callbackURL=/auth/complete (lines 268, 282). The callbackURL is hardcoded to /auth/complete. The /auth/complete route reads the epds_auth_flow cookie to get the flow_id and resume the AT Protocol flow. This works IF the cookie is set before the social login redirect. The cookie IS set in the GET /oauth/authorize handler (line 108). However, after the social OAuth round-trip (Google/GitHub → callback → better-auth → /auth/complete), the browser may have lost the epds_auth_flow cookie if the social provider's redirect clears cookies or if SameSite=Lax causes the cookie to not be sent on the cross-site redirect back. This is a known issue with SameSite=Lax and OAuth redirects. The flow_id should be passed as a query param in the callbackURL as a fallback: callbackURL=/auth/complete?flow_id=${flowId}.

title: Fix: ePDS-7ny is a duplicate of ePDS-ooq — same title and description filed twice
description: Review of cleanup commits 781b4f4 and 21acc7c found: ePDS-7ny ('VALID_HANDLE_MODES constant and HandleMode type defined inside request handler scope') is an exact duplicate of ePDS-ooq, filed in the same original review batch. Both have identical titles, descriptions, and evidence. One should be closed as a duplicate. Recommend closing ePDS-7ny and keeping ePDS-ooq (which has the discovered-from dependency on ePDS-d3x). Evidence: .beads/issues.jsonl — both entries have identical title and description text.

title: Fix: choose-handle page missing aria-live region for screen reader accessibility (from ePDS-1k2.4)
description:
Review of ePDS-1k2.4 found: The handle status area (id='handle-status') in the choose-handle page (packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts, line 392) dynamically updates with availability feedback ('Checking...', '✓ Available!', '✗ Already taken') but has no `aria-live` attribute. Screen reader users will not hear these updates. Evidence (line 392): ```html
``` Fix: Add `aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"` to the status div: ```html
``` Additionally, the input field lacks `aria-describedby="handle-status"` to associate the status with the input for screen readers.

title: Build auth_flow table and AT Protocol bridge route
description:
## Context

Phase 2 core of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md` Phase 2.

## Problem

The AT Protocol OAuth flow starts with a PAR `request_uri` that must survive the authentication process and arrive at pds-core's `/oauth/magic-callback`. Currently this is threaded through HTML hidden form fields and stored in the `magic_link_token` DB row. With better-auth, the OTP/social-login flow is opaque — we cannot embed `request_uri` in better-auth's internal state.

## Solution

### auth_flow table

Add a new `auth_flow` table to the auth-service SQLite:

```sql
CREATE TABLE auth_flow (
  flow_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  request_uri TEXT NOT NULL,
  client_id TEXT,
  email TEXT,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  expires_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
```

Cleanup: delete expired rows on the same 5-minute interval as existing token cleanup.

### Bridge route: auth-service `GET /auth/complete`

This is the route better-auth redirects to after successful authentication. It is the `callbackURL` passed to better-auth's sign-in methods.

Steps:
1. Read `magic_auth_flow` cookie → get flow_id
2. Look up auth_flow row → get request_uri, client_id
3. Get better-auth session via `auth.api.getSession({ headers })`
4. Extract verified email from session
5. Check consent needed (reuse existing client_logins logic)
6. If consent needed → redirect to auth-service `/auth/consent?flow_id=...`
7. Otherwise → build HMAC-signed redirect URL:
   - Compute HMAC-SHA256(request_uri || email || approved || new_account || ts, MAGIC_CALLBACK_SECRET)
   - Redirect to pds-core `/oauth/magic-callback?...&ts=...&sig=...`
8. Delete auth_flow row + clear cookie

## Files to create

- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts` — the bridge route

## Files to modify

- `packages/shared/src/db.ts` — add auth_flow table creation/migration + CRUD methods
- `packages/auth-service/src/index.ts` — mount the /auth/complete route

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] auth_flow table created in auth-service SQLite
- [ ] Expired auth_flow rows cleaned up periodically
- [ ] Bridge route reads better-auth session and extracts verified email
- [ ] Bridge route produces HMAC-signed redirect to pds-core /oauth/magic-callback
- [ ] Consent flow still works (redirect to /auth/consent when needed)
- [ ] Cookie set/cleared correctly
close_reason: Added auth_flow table (v6 migration) to shared/db.ts with createAuthFlow/getAuthFlow/deleteAuthFlow/cleanupExpiredAuthFlows CRUD methods. Created packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts bridge route that reads magic_auth_flow cookie, validates auth_flow record, gets better-auth session via fromNodeHeaders, determines isNewAccount via PDS internal endpoint, handles consent redirect, and issues HMAC-signed redirect to pds-core /oauth/magic-callback. Mounted in index.ts and wired cleanup in context.ts. 67 tests pass.

title: OTP email branding: support per-client templates
description:
The OTP email is currently hardcoded with Ma Earth branding. This needs to be fixed in one of two ways:

## Option A — Certified branding (simple)
Replace the Ma Earth branding with Certified branding as the default. Low effort, works for all clients but is not customisable.

## Option B — Per-client email templates (extensible)
Allow each trusted client app to provide its own email templates, which the auth service retrieves over HTTP at send time (or caches). The client would expose a well-known URL (e.g. `/.well-known/email-templates/otp.html`) that the auth service fetches and fills in with template variables (email address, OTP code, expiry, etc.).

This is analogous to how the HyperCerts PDS fork handles custom branding for trusted clients — the auth service fetches branding assets from the client's metadata URL.

## Considerations
- Option B requires a mechanism for the auth service to know which client triggered the OTP send (the `auth_flow` row already records the `client_id`/`request_uri`, so the client can be identified)
- Template fetching should be cached and have a timeout to avoid blocking OTP delivery
- Fallback to Certified default branding if the client provides no template
- Security: templates must be fetched only from trusted clients (already registered); content should be sanitised or restricted to a safe subset of HTML
- Option A can be done immediately as a stop-gap; Option B is the longer-term solution

## Reference
See how custom branding is handled in the HyperCerts PDS fork for the trusted client pattern.
close_reason: Already fully implemented. sender.ts fetches email_template_uri from client metadata (resolveClientMetadata), renders it with Mustache-style conditionals (renderTemplate), and falls back to default Certified templates. better-auth.ts resolves clientId from the auth_flow cookie and passes it to sendOtpCode. maearth-demo already serves email_template_uri pointing to /public/email-template.html with Ma Earth branding and {{code}}/{{app_name}}/{{is_new_user}} placeholders. The Ma Earth branded OTP email is the feature working correctly.

title: No end-to-end test for random-mode handle path in complete.ts
description: Review of 0962a39 found: The new 'random' handle_mode branch in complete.ts (step 5a) has no integration or unit test covering the full path: (1) auth_flow with handleMode='random' exists, (2) /auth/complete is called, (3) auth_flow is deleted, (4) cookie is cleared, (5) HMAC-signed redirect to /oauth/epds-callback is issued WITHOUT a handle param, (6) pds-core falls into the random-handle generation branch. The login-page.test.ts only tests DB storage of handle modes. The complete.ts route handler has no test file at all (noted in testing-gaps.md as integration-level). This means the random-mode path could silently regress. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/__tests__/ has no complete.test.ts; the new tests in login-page.test.ts only cover DB round-trips.

title: Fix: dynamic role=alert assignment may not be announced by older screen readers (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: the error/success admonition div starts with no role attribute and style='display:none'. The showError() and showSuccess() JS functions call errorEl.setAttribute('role', 'alert'/'status') before setting display='flex'. 

The problem: NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver register ARIA live regions at page load time. Dynamically adding role='alert' to an element that was not a live region at load time is not reliably announced by all screen reader/browser combinations. The W3C ARIA authoring practices recommend having the live region present in the DOM from page load (even if empty/hidden) so screen readers register it.

Evidence: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/alert/ — 'It is important to note that the alert role is not appropriate for dialog boxes. Instead, use the alertdialog role.'  Also: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/alert_role — 'Do not dynamically add role=alert to an element.'

Fix: Add role='alert' aria-live='assertive' aria-atomic='true' to the #error-msg div in the initial HTML (alongside style='display:none'). Remove the setAttribute/removeAttribute calls from showError/showSuccess/clearError. Screen readers will then announce content changes when the element becomes visible.

title: Fix: race condition — tryRandomHandle can overwrite user's typed input after they start typing (from ePDS-cg8)
description: Review of 4246a86 found: tryRandomHandle() sets input.value = local (line 594) and later sets isAvailable = true and input.value is already set. If the user starts typing in the handle input while a tryRandomHandle fetch is in-flight (the button is disabled but the input is not), the fetch completion at line 603-606 sets isAvailable = true and calls updateSubmit() — but the input.value was already set to the random handle at line 594 before the fetch, so the user's typed value is not overwritten by the fetch itself. However, if tryRandomHandle retries (collision path, line 609), it calls randomLocalPart() again and sets input.value to a new random value, overwriting whatever the user typed. The fix is to abort tryRandomHandle's in-flight fetch when the user types (similar to how the input listener aborts currentAbort), or to check whether the input value has changed from the expected random value before updating isAvailable. Evidence: choose-handle.ts lines 593-609.

title: Flow 2 (app has login button, no login_hint) is untested and likely broken
description:
Flow 2 — where the client app has a simple 'Login' button and no email form of its own — has never been tested end-to-end. Only Flow 1 (login_hint passed via PAR) has been verified working.

## What Flow 2 is supposed to do

1. Client redirects to /oauth/authorize with no login_hint
2. Auth server renders the email input form (step-email div, visible by default)
3. User enters email, submits form
4. JS calls sendOtp(email) → POST /api/auth/email-otp/send-verification-otp
5. On success, JS calls showOtpStep(email) to transition to OTP input
6. User enters OTP code
7. JS calls verifyOtp(email, otp) → POST /api/auth/sign-in/email-otp
8. On success, redirects to /auth/complete
9. /auth/complete reads magic_auth_flow cookie → issues AT Protocol auth code via /oauth/magic-callback
10. Client exchanges code for tokens

## Current code state

The email form and JS transition path exists in login-page.ts (step-email div rendered
visible when no loginHint, form-send-otp submit handler calls sendOtp → showOtpStep).
However it has not been exercised and may have issues at any step.

## What needs investigating / testing

- Does a client without login_hint successfully redirect to /oauth/authorize?
- Does the email form render correctly?
- Does sendOtp succeed (better-auth endpoint reachable, correct request format)?
- Does showOtpStep transition work (CSS classes, currentEmail set correctly)?
- Does verifyOtp succeed and redirect to /auth/complete?
- Does /auth/complete correctly pick up the magic_auth_flow cookie in this path?
- Does magic-callback create the PDS account correctly for a new user?
- Does the whole token exchange complete?

## Reference implementation

The maearth-demo app at /data/projects/maearth-demo only implements Flow 1.
To test Flow 2, either modify maearth-demo to skip the login_hint, or use a
separate minimal client (e.g. curl + browser) that redirects to /oauth/authorize
without login_hint.
close_reason: Tested and working.

title: Fix: title panel h1 CSS selector mismatch — .title class has no CSS definition (from ePDS-aq2.4)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.4 found: The HTML renders `

` (line 711) but the CSS targets `.title-panel h1` (line 403) — not `.title`. The `.title` class has no CSS definition anywhere in the stylesheet. Evidence: - Line 711: `

Sign in with Certified

` - Line 403: `.title-panel h1 { font-size: 20px; ... }` — targets h1 by tag, not by class - No `.title { ... }` rule exists in the stylesheet The styling still works because `.title-panel h1` matches any h1 inside .title-panel. However: 1. The `.title` class is dead markup — it does nothing 2. The ePDS-aq2.4 spec says to add `.title { ... }` CSS rules (font-size: 20px, font-weight: 600, etc.) 3. The CSS selector approach is inconsistent — the spec intended a `.title` class with its own rules, not a descendant selector Fix: Either add a `.title { ... }` CSS rule matching the spec, or remove the `class="title"` from the h1 (since the descendant selector already works). The former is preferred for consistency with the spec.

title: Fix: 'a login form is displayed' step asserts wrong h1 text on /account/login page (from ePDS-geiz)
description:
Review of ePDS-geiz found: account.steps.ts lines 111-117 asserts: await expect(this.page.locator('#email')).toBeVisible() const title = this.page.locator('h1') await expect(title).toContainText('Account Settings') But the /account/login page (account-login.ts line 148) renders:

Account Settings

This is actually correct — the login page DOES have h1 'Account Settings'. However, after the user submits the email form (POST /account/send-otp), the server renders the OTP form page which has:

Enter your code

The step is checking the login form page (before submission), so the h1 assertion is correct at that point. This is a false alarm — no fix needed. However, there is a related style issue: the step is checking the h1 on the email form page, but the Gherkin says 'a login form is displayed (separate from the OAuth flow)'. The h1 check is redundant since #email visibility already confirms we are on the login form. Consider removing the h1 assertion to make the step more focused.

title: Test gap: recovery.test.ts asserts clientId is null — test validates buggy behaviour
description:
## Finding

`packages/auth-service/src/__tests__/recovery.test.ts` lines 91-101 contain:

```ts
it('creates auth_flow with requestUri when backup email is found', () => {
  const flowId = randomBytes(16).toString('hex')
  const requestUri = 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:request_uri:recovery-test'

  db.createAuthFlow({
    flowId,
    requestUri,
    clientId: null,   // <-- mirrors the bug in recovery.ts:118
    expiresAt: Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000,
  })

  const flow = db.getAuthFlow(flowId)
  expect(flow).toBeDefined()
  expect(flow!.requestUri).toBe(requestUri)
  expect(flow!.clientId).toBeNull()   // <-- asserts null, which is the bug
})
```

This test was written to match the current (buggy) behaviour where `clientId: null` is passed. When the bug in `recovery.ts:118` is fixed (issue ePDS-62m), this test must be updated to assert that `clientId` is stored correctly when available.

The test also does not test the route handler directly — it only tests the DB layer. A route-level test that verifies the POST /auth/recover handler passes `clientId` through to `createAuthFlow` would catch this class of bug.

## Action

When fixing ePDS-62m, also update this test to:
1. Assert `flow!.clientId` equals the clientId that was available (not null)
2. Add a route-level test (using the mock req/res pattern already in consent.test.ts) that verifies the POST handler stores clientId correctly

- session.ts: Copy HMAC-signed cookie sessions. Remove the 2FA 'verified' field from UserSession interface and all references to it.

title: Plan gap: fallback auth_flow creation block (recovery.ts:111-127) must be deleted, not just modified
description:
## Finding

The refactoring plan says POST `/auth/recover` should 'read from cookie → DB instead of req.body.request_uri / req.body.client_id'. However, the plan does not explicitly say to **delete** the fallback auth_flow creation block at recovery.ts:111-127:

```ts
let flowId = req.cookies[AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE] as string | undefined
if (!flowId || !ctx.db.getAuthFlow(flowId)) {
  const { randomBytes } = await import('node:crypto')
  flowId = randomBytes(16).toString('hex')
  ctx.db.createAuthFlow({
    flowId,
    requestUri,          // <-- currently from req.body.request_uri
    clientId: null,
    expiresAt: Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000,
  })
  res.cookie(AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE, flowId, { ... })
}
```

After the refactor, `requestUri` is no longer in `req.body`. If this block is left in place (even with the clientId bug fixed), it would call `createAuthFlow` with `requestUri: undefined` — silently storing a corrupt row. The DB column is NOT NULL, so this would throw a SQLite constraint error at runtime, crashing the handler.

## Impact

If a worker follows the plan and only changes where `requestUri`/`clientId` are read from (cookie vs body) without explicitly removing this block, the fallback creation path becomes a runtime crash.

## Required fix

The plan must explicitly state: **delete** the entire fallback auth_flow creation block (lines 111-127). Under the new model, if the cookie is absent or the flow is expired, the POST handler returns a clear error page — it does not attempt to create a new flow.

title: Update consent screen to use auth_flow table
description:
## Context

Phase 2 of the better-auth migration plan. See `better-auth-migration-plan.md` Phase 2.4.

## Problem

The current consent screen (`routes/consent.ts`) reads `request_uri` directly from query parameters. With the new auth_flow-based architecture, the request_uri is stored in the `auth_flow` table, keyed by flow_id.

## Solution

Update `GET/POST /auth/consent` on the auth-service to:
- Read `flow_id` from query parameter (or `magic_auth_flow` cookie) instead of `request_uri` directly
- Look up `auth_flow` row to get `request_uri` and `client_id`
- After consent is granted, build HMAC-signed redirect URL to pds-core `/oauth/magic-callback`
- Delete the old `routes/consent.ts` implementation (replace in-place)

## Files to modify

- `packages/auth-service/src/routes/consent.ts` — rewrite to use auth_flow table

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Consent screen reads request_uri from auth_flow table via flow_id
- [ ] After consent, redirect is HMAC-signed
- [ ] First-time consent tracking (client_logins table) still works
- [ ] Consent screen still displays client branding
close_reason: Updated consent screen to support flow_id mode: GET reads request_uri/client_id from auth_flow table via flow_id query param. POST approves by looking up auth_flow, deletes the row after use, and builds HMAC-signed redirect to pds-core /oauth/magic-callback. Legacy direct request_uri mode retained for backward compat. complete.ts updated to pass flow_id to consent instead of request_uri, deferring auth_flow cleanup. 12 tests pass.

title: Fix OTP step button layout: stack vertically (primary full-width, secondary side-by-side)
description: The 3 buttons in the OTP step (Sign in with Certified, Resend code, Use different email) are in a single flex-row-reverse row that wraps awkwardly. Change to: primary button full-width on top, then the two secondary buttons side by side below. Files: packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts only.
