title: Fix: recovery .link-btn has padding:0 — touch target too small on mobile (from ePDS-aq2.5)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.5 found: The .link-btn CSS rule in recovery.ts (line 636) sets padding: 0, which means the touch target for 'Back to sign in' and 'Resend Code' links is only the text height (~20px). WCAG 2.5.5 recommends a minimum 44×44px touch target.

Evidence:
- recovery.ts line 636: padding: 0; (inside .link-btn rule)
- The same issue exists in login-page.ts (line 565: padding: 0)

The .link-btn elements appear in:
1. .form-actions row: 'Back to sign in' link (line 246)
2. .otp-links row: 'Resend Code' button and 'Back to sign in' link (lines 319, 321)

These are the only interactive elements in those rows, so small touch targets are a real usability issue on mobile.

Fix: Add padding: 8px 0 (or similar) to .link-btn to increase the touch target height to at least 36px, while keeping the visual appearance the same. This fix should be applied to both recovery.ts and login-page.ts for consistency. Note: this is a pre-existing issue in login-page.ts that was copied into recovery.ts.

title: Fix: renderNoAccountPage() response missing HTTP status code (from ePDS-01p.3)
description: Review of ePDS-01p.3 found: In account-settings.ts line 69, when no PDS account is found for the session email, the response is sent as 'res.type("html").send(renderNoAccountPage())' without setting an HTTP status code. This defaults to 200 OK, which is semantically incorrect for an error page and may confuse clients, monitoring tools, or browser caching. The 503 branch (PDS unavailable) correctly uses res.status(503), but the no-account branch silently returns 200. Fix: add an appropriate status code — either 403 Forbidden (the session is valid but the user has no account) or 404 Not Found. Suggested: res.status(403).type("html").send(renderNoAccountPage()). Evidence: account-settings.ts line 69 — no .status() call before .type('html').send(...).

title: Fix: dark mode body background overridden by light-mode rule ordering (from ePDS-wae.2)
description: The CSS has two body background rules: (1) body { background: var(--color-contrast-0); } at line ~364 and (2) @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) { body { background: white; } } at line ~371. In dark mode, rule (1) correctly uses --color-contrast-0 which resolves to hsl(265 20% 6%) — a near-black. However, when AUTH_BACKGROUND_COLOR is set, the bgColorStyle injects a <style>body { background: ... !important; }</style> block. The !important correctly overrides both rules. But if a client's background_color is set via OAuth metadata (b.background_color), it is NOT injected into the bgColorStyle block — it is only set via --color-panel (which only affects the panel, not body). The body background_color from client OAuth metadata is silently ignored. Document this limitation or implement it.

title: Bug: recovery GET requires request_uri query param but plan says cookie should be sufficient
description:
## Finding

`packages/auth-service/src/routes/recovery.ts` lines 42-44 return HTTP 400 if `request_uri` is absent from the query string:

```ts
if (!requestUri) {
  res.status(400).send(renderError('Missing request_uri parameter'))
  return
}
```

The login page recovery link at `login-page.ts:368` currently passes `request_uri` and `client_id` as query params to work around this:

```ts
<a href="/auth/recover?request_uri=${...}\&client_id=${...}" ...>
```

This is the current working state, but it means:

1. The recovery page is reachable only via the login page link (which includes the params). Direct navigation or a stale link without params returns 400.
2. The plan to simplify the recovery link to just `/auth/recover` (change #5) would break the GET handler unless change #4 (read from cookie instead) is implemented first. These two changes are tightly coupled and must be implemented atomically.
3. The fallback at lines 47-51 (`getAuthFlowByRequestUri`) only runs when `clientId` is missing but `requestUri` is present — it never fires when `requestUri` itself is absent.

## Not a regression — this is the current design

This is not a regression from any recent change. It is a pre-existing coupling that the refactoring plan must address carefully. The plan correctly identifies this (change #4), but the implementation must ensure the GET handler is updated before or simultaneously with simplifying the login page link (change #5). If done in two separate commits, the intermediate state would break recovery.

title: Prevent flash of email login form when login_hint is present
description:
When /oauth/authorize is called with a login_hint email, the current implementation renders the full login page (including the email input step) and then client-side JS transitions to the OTP step after sending the OTP. This causes a brief flash of the email input form before the OTP step appears, which is confusing to users who already entered their email in the client app.

Fix: when login_hint is a valid email, send the OTP server-side during the GET /oauth/authorize handler and render the page already in the OTP step (hiding the email step entirely via HTML/CSS). No client-side transition needed — the OTP step should be immediately visible with no flash.
notes:
When this issue is resolved, also remove the 'known issue: brief flash of email form' caveat from README.md (Flow 1 description) and from docs/flows.md.

A partial fix exists in git stash: 'atproto-ke8: partial flash fix (server-side step visibility, OTP still client-side)'. What it achieves: renders step-email hidden and step-otp visible server-side when login_hint is present, pre-fills the masked email subtitle, pre-fills the hidden email input, and shows the recovery link. The flash is eliminated visually. What it does NOT do: OTP is still sent client-side via JS on page load (sendOtp called in DOMContentLoaded). The full fix requires moving OTP dispatch into the server-side GET handler before rendering the page. See ePDS-trf for investigation into that approach.
close_reason: Implemented Method-Aware Hybrid approach: server-side initialStep rendering (no flash), client-side POST auto-send with accurate subtitle UX, otpAlreadySent flag for duplicate GET protection. Design doc and README/flows.md caveats updated.

title: Include plaintext email part alongside HTML in OTP emails
description: OTP emails currently only have an HTML MIME part. For improved accessibility (screen readers, text-only email clients), a plaintext alternative should be included as a multipart/alternative MIME part alongside the HTML.

title: Rename MagicLink types and DB methods → EpdsVerification
description:
Rename the MagicLink-prefixed types, interfaces, DB methods, function names, AND the SQL table.

Since there are no production deployments, rename the SQL table too:
- magic_link_token → verification_token (in CREATE TABLE, indexes, all SQL queries)
- Update the migration version accordingly

Types/interfaces to rename:
- MagicLinkConfig → EpdsLinkConfig (packages/shared/src/types.ts)
- MagicLinkTokenRow → VerificationTokenRow (packages/shared/src/db.ts)
- magicLink config key → verificationLink (packages/shared/src/types.ts)

Functions to rename:
- generateMagicLinkToken() → generateVerificationToken() (packages/shared/src/crypto.ts)
- createMagicLinkToken() → createVerificationToken() (packages/shared/src/db.ts)
- getMagicLinkToken() → getVerificationToken() (packages/shared/src/db.ts)
- markMagicLinkTokenUsed() → markVerificationTokenUsed() (packages/shared/src/db.ts)
- getMagicLinkTokenByCsrf() → getVerificationTokenByCsrf() (packages/shared/src/db.ts)

SQL to rename:
- Table: magic_link_token → verification_token
- Index: idx_mlt_email → idx_vt_email
- Index: idx_mlt_expires → idx_vt_expires
- All SQL queries referencing the old table name

Exports to update:

Consumers to update:

- packages/auth-service/src/routes/consent.ts — comment about magic-link OTP path
- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts — comments about magic link

Tests to update:
- packages/shared/src/__tests__/crypto.test.ts
- packages/shared/src/__tests__/db.test.ts
notes: Workflow: after making changes, run pnpm typecheck && pnpm test. Commit on success. Do NOT push to GitHub.
close_reason: Renamed types, DB methods, SQL table, indexes, config keys, and comments. Typecheck + tests pass.

title: Rename magic-pds → ePDS and @magic-pds/ → @certified-app/ everywhere
description:
Rename magic-pds → ePDS and @magic-pds/ → @certified-app/ everywhere.

Scope:
1. Package names: @magic-pds/shared → @certified-app/shared, @magic-pds/auth-service → @certified-app/auth-service, @magic-pds/pds-core → @certified-app/pds-core
2. Root package.json name: magic-pds → epds
3. Class MagicPdsDb → EpdsDb (in db.ts and all import sites)
4. SQLite filename: magic-pds.sqlite → epds.sqlite (Dockerfile.auth, default paths in index.ts and better-auth.ts)
5. String 'magic-pds' in source/config (pds-core health endpoint, etc.)
6. Docs: AGENTS.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/development.md, docs/configuration.md
7. Leave better-auth-migration-plan.md alone (historical doc with external URL)
8. pnpm-lock.yaml will be regenerated by pnpm install after package.json changes
close_reason: All occurrences renamed: package names → @certified-app/, root name → epds, MagicPdsDb → EpdsDb, sqlite path → epds.sqlite, Docker tag, log messages, health endpoint, docs. 97 tests pass.

title: Plan gap: POST /auth/recover/verify input validation guard checks req.body.request_uri — will always 400 after refactor
description:
## Finding `POST /auth/recover/verify` has an input validation guard at recovery.ts:178: ```ts const requestUri = req.body.request_uri as string // ... if (!code || !email || !requestUri) { res.status(400).send('

Missing required fields.

') return } ``` After the refactor, `request_uri` is removed from the form body. `req.body.request_uri` will always be `undefined`. The guard `!requestUri` will always be truthy, so **every OTP verification attempt will return 400** with 'Missing required fields.' — completely breaking the verify handler. ## Impact This is a silent breakage: the handler appears to work (no crash), but every user who submits their OTP code gets a 400 error with no explanation. The refactoring plan does not mention updating this guard. ## Required fix The plan must explicitly state: update the validation guard in POST `/auth/recover/verify` to remove `requestUri` from the check. After the refactor, the guard should only check `!code || !email`. The `requestUri` (and `clientId`) are obtained from the DB lookup that follows, and the 'no valid flow' error handles the missing-flow case separately.

title: Fix: tryRandomHandle treats service errors as handle collisions, burning all retries (from ePDS-cg8)
description: Review of 4246a86 found: In the client-side tryRandomHandle() function (choose-handle.ts line 601-610), the .then() branch only checks data.available === true for success; all other responses (including data.error === 'service_unavailable' or data.error === 'invalid_format') fall into the else branch which retries as if the handle is taken. If the PDS is down or /api/check-handle returns a service error, all 3 retries are consumed treating each error as a collision, and the user sees 'Could not find a free handle. Try again.' instead of a service error message. The fix is to check for data.error in the .then() branch and surface it as a service error (re-enable button, show error message) rather than retrying. Evidence: choose-handle.ts lines 601-610.

title: Fix: .layout min-width:100vw causes horizontal scrollbar (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The .layout CSS rule (line 338) sets `min-width: 100vw`. On pages with a vertical scrollbar, `100vw` includes the scrollbar width, causing the layout to be wider than the viewport and triggering a horizontal scrollbar.

Evidence:
- Line 338: `min-width: 100vw;` on .layout
- The Bluesky reference (layout-title-page.tsx) uses Tailwind class `min-w-screen` which maps to `min-width: 100vw` — however, Bluesky's app is a SPA that controls scrollbar behavior. For a server-rendered page with potential content overflow, this causes a horizontal scrollbar on Windows/Linux where scrollbars take up space.

Fix: Replace `min-width: 100vw` with `width: 100%` on .layout. The body already has `min-height: 100vh` and `display: flex`, so .layout will naturally fill the available width.

title: Docs: migration v8 (handle_mode column) missing version comment
description:
Review of the handle mode system found: in packages/shared/src/db.ts, all migrations have a version comment (e.g. '// v1: Initial schema', '// v2: Add code_hash column for OTP support', etc.) except migration v8 which adds the handle_mode column to auth_flow:

  () => {
    this.db.exec(`ALTER TABLE auth_flow ADD COLUMN handle_mode TEXT;`)
  },

This migration is missing its '// v8: ...' comment, making it harder to understand the migration history at a glance.

The fix: add a comment like '// v8: Add handle_mode column to auth_flow for optional handle selection mode'.

Evidence: packages/shared/src/db.ts lines 181-183.

title: Fix: drift signOut does not forward Set-Cookie to Express res (from ePDS-01p.3)
description:
Review of ePDS-01p.3 found: the drift-detection path in requireBetterAuth calls auth.api.signOut() but never forwards the Set-Cookie header from the better-auth Response object to the Express res. The session cookie is therefore NOT cleared in the browser.

Evidence: account-settings.ts lines 69-74:
  await auth.api.signOut({ headers: fromNodeHeaders(req.headers) })
  res.redirect(303, '/account/login')

The signOut call returns a Response with Set-Cookie headers that expire the session cookie. These headers are never read or forwarded to res. Compare with account-login.ts lines 101-104 which correctly does:
  const setCookie = response.headers.get('set-cookie')
  if (setCookie) { res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', setCookie) }

Fix: capture the return value of auth.api.signOut() and forward its Set-Cookie header to res before redirecting, matching the pattern in account-login.ts.

Note: the existing /account/logout (lines 280-286) and /account/delete (lines 401-404) routes have the same pre-existing bug and should be fixed in the same pass.

title: Fix: autofocus on email input fires when OTP step is initially shown (from ePDS-aq2.1)
description:
Review of ePDS-aq2.1 found: The email input (line 749) has `autofocus` unconditionally. When `initialStep === 'otp'` (i.e. a login_hint was provided), the email step div is hidden (`class="step-email hidden"`) but the autofocus attribute still fires on the hidden input. This means:
1. The OTP code input does not receive focus on initial load (the JS auto-send fires but doesn't call .focus() on the code input for the initial server-rendered OTP step)
2. Some browsers may scroll to or attempt to focus the hidden email input

Evidence:
- Line 749: `<input ... autofocus` — unconditional
- Line 738: `class="step-email${opts.initialStep === 'otp' ? ' hidden' : ''}"` — hidden when OTP step
- Line 972-984: JS auto-send block does NOT call `document.getElementById('code').focus()` after the OTP send succeeds on initial load

Fix: Make autofocus conditional: only add the autofocus attribute when `opts.initialStep !== 'otp'`. When initialStep is 'otp', add autofocus to the code input instead, or call .focus() in the JS auto-send success handler.

title: Epic: Resolve CodeRabbit review for PR #8
description: Resolve open CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #8. 1 actionable comment (security fix for domain boundary matching in /tls-check). 1 comment skipped (Caddyfile ask URL — env var default is intentional).

title: Tighten domain boundary matching in /tls-check to prevent sibling domain spoofing
description:
## Files
- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (modify)

## What to do
In the `checkHandleRoute()` function (~line 478), the current domain matching uses `domain.endsWith(avail)` which allows sibling domains (e.g. `evil-example.com` matches `example.com`). Fix this by:

1. Normalize the incoming `domain` query param before any checks:
   ```ts
   const normalizedDomain = domain.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\.$/, '')
   ```
   Place this right after the `typeof domain !== 'string'` guard.

2. Replace the `find` + `endsWith` check with `some` + proper boundary matching:
   ```ts
   const isHostedHandle = pds.ctx.cfg.identity.serviceHandleDomains.some(
     (avail: string) => {
       const normalizedAvail = avail.toLowerCase()
       return (
         normalizedDomain === normalizedAvail ||
         normalizedDomain.endsWith(`.${normalizedAvail}`)
       )
     },
   )
   ```

3. Use `normalizedDomain` (instead of raw `domain`) in the `getAccount()` call below:
   ```ts
   const account = await pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccount(normalizedDomain)
   ```

4. Also use `normalizedDomain` in the hostname/auth checks above:
   ```ts
   if (normalizedDomain === pds.ctx.cfg.service.hostname || normalizedDomain === authHostname) {
   ```

## Don't
- Change any other function or route
- Add new dependencies
- Modify the Caddyfile
- Change the response shapes or status codes

title: Fix: POST /auth/choose-handle shows 'handle taken' error when PDS API returns non-OK status (from ePDS-1k2.4)
Distinguish first-time sign-up from subsequent logins in OTP emails
description: When a user receives an OTP code, the email should differ depending on whether this is their first time signing up or a subsequent login. A welcome-style email for first sign-up and a simpler 'here is your login code' email for returning users would improve the user experience and reduce confusion.
close_reason: Fixed in signup-vs-login-otp-email branch

title: Restructure docs: README for frontend devs, internals in docs/
description:
The README.md currently mixes two audiences:
1. Frontend developers building client apps against the ePDS
2. Contributors working on the ePDS itself

Split these concerns:
- README.md should focus purely on helping frontend developers understand how to build against the ePDS: the two login flows, PAR/OAuth endpoints, login_hint convention, client metadata, callback handling, etc.
- Internal architecture, package structure, deployment, configuration, and development setup should move to docs/ subdirectory (e.g. docs/architecture.md, docs/deployment.md, docs/development.md)

The Mermaid sequence diagrams (ePDS-y1l) should go in docs/ as part of this restructure.
close_reason: README rewritten for frontend devs; architecture, deployment, development, configuration, and flows docs created in docs/

title: Design gap: auth_flow TTL is 10 min but cookie maxAge is also 10 min — race condition on slow recovery flows
description:
## Finding

Both the auth_flow DB row and the `epds_auth_flow` cookie are set to expire in 10 minutes (`AUTH_FLOW_TTL_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000`). They are created at the same moment in `login-page.ts` (lines 108, 121-126).

Under the proposed refactoring, the recovery flow would rely entirely on the cookie + DB row. The recovery flow involves:

1. User visits login page → cookie + DB row created (T=0)
2. User clicks 'Recover with backup email' → GET /auth/recover (T+N)
3. User enters backup email → POST /auth/recover (T+M)
4. User receives email, reads it, enters OTP → POST /auth/recover/verify (T+P)

If steps 2-4 take more than 10 minutes total (slow email delivery, user distracted), both the cookie and the DB row expire simultaneously. The user then hits /auth/complete and gets a 400 with no recovery path.

## Current state vs proposed state

Currently, the hidden form fields (`request_uri`, `client_id`) survive cookie/DB expiry because they are embedded in the HTML form. Under the proposed refactoring (removing hidden fields), there is no fallback — expiry means a hard failure.

## Recommendation

The plan should either:
(a) Extend the auth_flow TTL for recovery flows (e.g. 30 minutes) when the recovery POST creates/reuses a flow, OR
(b) Explicitly document that 10 minutes is the hard limit and the error message should direct users to restart from the login page, OR  
(c) Refresh the cookie and DB row expiry on each recovery POST step (sliding window).

This is not a regression introduced by the refactoring — it exists today — but the refactoring removes the hidden-field safety net that currently masks the problem.

title: Set up ESLint for TypeScript linting
description: Add ESLint to the monorepo with typescript-eslint. Include a lint script in package.json, a flat config (eslint.config.js), and rules aligned with the project's code style conventions in AGENTS.md (strict mode, type imports, named exports, etc). Fix any lint errors across the codebase.
close_reason: ESLint configured: flat config with typescript-eslint strict type-checked, eslint-config-prettier, no-default-export. All lint errors fixed (catch unknown, unused imports/vars, require→import, inline suppressions for better-auth/atproto any types). Zero errors, zero warnings.

title: Epic: E2E test framework — cucumber-js + Playwright against Railway
description:
## Goal
Wire up a working E2E BDD test framework (cucumber-js + Playwright + tsx) that runs the existing passwordless-authentication.feature against live Railway services. Browser tests run headed so the developer can watch.

## Context
- 15 Gherkin .feature files (86 scenarios) already exist in features/
- Three Railway services are live and healthy:
  - PDS core: https://karmas-e2e-pds.up.railway.app
  - Auth service: https://certified-e2e-auth.up.railway.app
  - Demo app: https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app
- No MailHog yet — OTP-dependent steps call this.pending() and skip gracefully
- No Docker-local environment — Railway-first, Docker comes later
- No CI workflow yet — local runs only for now
- docs/design/e2e-testing.md specifies tests live in e2e/ directory

## Directory layout
```
features/                          # .feature files (already exist, untouched)
  passwordless-authentication.feature
  ...14 more...
e2e/                               # all test infrastructure (new)
  support/
    env.ts
    world.ts
    hooks.ts
  step-definitions/
    common.steps.ts
    auth.steps.ts
    email.steps.ts
  cucumber.mjs
  tsconfig.e2e.json
```

## Success
Running 'pnpm test:e2e' opens a browser, navigates to the demo app, clicks sign in, follows the OAuth redirect to the auth service, enters an email, sees the OTP form appear, then the MailHog-dependent steps go pending. No red failures. No feature files touched.

## Out of scope
- Docker-local environment (dnsmasq, Caddy internal CA, compose overlay)
- CI/GitHub Actions workflows
- Step definitions for any feature file other than passwordless-authentication.feature
- MailHog deployment or OTP retrieval
- Stagehand integration (plain Playwright for now)
- Fixing hardcoded https:// references (not needed for Railway)

title: Install dependencies and configure cucumber-js runner
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- e2e/cucumber.mjs (create)

## What to do
Add three devDependencies to root package.json:
- `@cucumber/cucumber` ^11.0.0
- `@playwright/test` ^1.50.0
- `tsx` ^4.0.0

Add two scripts to root package.json:
- `"test:e2e": "TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js --config e2e/cucumber.mjs"`
- `"test:e2e:ci": "E2E_HEADLESS=true TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/cucumber-js --config e2e/cucumber.mjs"`

Create e2e/cucumber.mjs:
```js
export default {
  paths: ['features/**/*.feature'],
  import: ['e2e/step-definitions/**/*.ts', 'e2e/support/**/*.ts'],
  format: ['progress-bar', 'html:reports/e2e.html'],
  publishQuiet: true,
  tags: 'not @manual',
}
```

Key details:
- Use `import:` NOT `require:` — the repo is ESM (`"type": "module"` in package.json)
- Use tsx as the ESM TypeScript loader, NOT ts-node (ts-node/register is CJS-only and will throw ERR_REQUIRE_ESM)
- Do NOT add a `requireModule` field — tsx handles TS via the node --import flag
- The `@playwright/test` package includes both the playwright library AND the expect API with auto-waiting matchers (toBeVisible, toHaveText, etc.). Plain `playwright` does NOT include expect.
- The `paths` field points to features/ (at root), but `import` points to e2e/ (step definitions and support)
- The `--config e2e/cucumber.mjs` flag tells cucumber-js to read config from the e2e/ directory

Run `pnpm install` after modifying package.json. Then run `npx playwright install chromium` to download the browser binary.

## Don't
- Don't add ts-node as a dependency
- Don't add plain `playwright` (use `@playwright/test` which includes it)
- Don't use `require:` or `requireModule:` in cucumber.mjs
- Don't add a playwright.config.ts — it's not needed (Playwright is used as a library, not a runner)
- Don't put cucumber.mjs at the repo root — it goes in e2e/
- Don't modify any existing scripts
close_reason: 0a9152a Install cucumber-js, playwright, tsx and configure e2e runner

title: Fix: e2e/ files excluded from ESLint projectService — pnpm lint fails (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: `pnpm lint` fails with 7 errors because the ESLint `projectService` config only knows about the root `tsconfig.json` (which covers only `packages/`). The `e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json` is standalone and not registered with ESLint's project service, so all 6 TypeScript files under `e2e/` and `e2e/cucumber.mjs` produce: 'was not found by the project service. Consider either including it in the tsconfig.json or including it in allowDefaultProject'.

Evidence: running `pnpm lint` produces 7 errors (exit code 1):
- e2e/cucumber.mjs
- e2e/step-definitions/auth.steps.ts
- e2e/step-definitions/common.steps.ts
- e2e/step-definitions/email.steps.ts
- e2e/support/env.ts
- e2e/support/hooks.ts
- e2e/support/world.ts

Fix: In `eslint.config.js`, add `e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json` to the `parserOptions.project` array (or use `allowDefaultProject` for `e2e/cucumber.mjs` which is not a TypeScript file). The TypeScript files can be covered by pointing ESLint at `e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json`. The `.mjs` file should either be added to ESLint ignores or covered via `allowDefaultProject`.

Constraint: Do NOT add `e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json` to the root `tsconfig.json` references array (per ePDS-nr36.2 spec).
close_reason: 12223b3 fix: register e2e tsconfig with ESLint and ignore cucumber.mjs

title: Fix: cucumber.mjs export default violates repo-wide no-default-exports ESLint rule (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: `e2e/cucumber.mjs` uses `export default { ... }` which violates the repo-wide ESLint rule `no-restricted-syntax` that bans `ExportDefaultDeclaration` (configured in `eslint.config.js` lines 65-71). This rule applies to all files not in the ignores list, and `e2e/` is not ignored.

Evidence: `eslint.config.js` has:
```js
'no-restricted-syntax': [
  'error',
  {
    selector: 'ExportDefaultDeclaration',
    message: 'Use named exports instead of default exports.',
  },
],
```

The cucumber-js config format requires a default export when using a `.mjs` config file. Fix options:
1. Add an ESLint override for `e2e/cucumber.mjs` that turns off `no-restricted-syntax` (similar to the `packages/demo/**` override that already exists for Next.js pages).
2. Rename to `cucumber.config.cjs` and use `module.exports` — but this conflicts with the ESM repo.
3. Add `e2e/cucumber.mjs` to the ESLint `ignores` array (simplest, since it's a config file not source code).

Note: This issue is secondary to ePDS-nr36.10 (project service error). Once the project service is fixed, this rule violation will surface.
close_reason: 12223b3 fix: register e2e tsconfig with ESLint and ignore cucumber.mjs

title: Fix: Scenario 6 refresh step navigates to demo URL instead of auth service (from ePDS-nr36)
Fix: 'enters an incorrect OTP code N times' uses fixed 500ms delay instead of waiting for error (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: The 'enters an incorrect OTP code {int} times' step in auth.steps.ts (lines 107-116) uses a fixed 500ms delay between OTP submission attempts:

  for (let i = 0; i < times; i++) {
    await this.page.fill('#code', '00000000')
    await this.page.click('#form-verify-otp .btn-primary')
    await this.page.waitForTimeout(500)
  }

The OTP verification is an async fetch call to the better-auth API. On Railway with cold-start latency, the server response can take more than 500ms. If the next iteration starts before the previous response arrives, the form may be in a submitting state (button disabled) and the fill/click may interact with stale state or be ignored.

The correct approach is to wait for the error message (#error-msg) to become visible after each submission before proceeding to the next iteration. This ensures each attempt completes before the next begins.

title: Fix: waitForLoadState('networkidle') in email submit steps uses 15s default timeout — too short for Railway cold starts (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: The 'the user enters {string} and submits' and 'the user enters {string} on the login page' steps (auth.steps.ts lines 28-44) and 'the user requests an OTP for {string}' step (lines 89-100) all call:

  await this.page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')

Playwright's waitForLoadState uses the page's default timeout, which is set to 15s in hooks.ts (this.page.setDefaultTimeout(15_000)). The OAuth redirect chain after email submission is: demo backend -> PDS PAR -> auth service. On Railway with cold-start latency, this chain can take 10-20+ seconds. If it exceeds 15s, the step fails with a timeout error before the subsequent #step-otp.active check (which has its own 30s timeout) even runs.

The networkidle wait is also unnecessary since the subsequent step already waits for the OTP form to appear with a 30s timeout. Removing the networkidle wait and relying on the OTP form assertion is more robust.

title: Fix: Scenario 1 OTP form check is skipped (not executed) because MailHog step precedes it in feature file (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: In passwordless-authentication.feature Scenario 1, the step order is:

  When the user enters "alice@example.com" and submits
  Then an OTP email arrives in the mail trap for "alice@example.com"  <- goes PENDING (no MailHog)
  And the email subject contains "Welcome" (new user)                  <- SKIPPED
  And the login page shows an OTP verification form                     <- SKIPPED  <-- KEY ASSERTION
  When the user enters the OTP code from the email                      <- SKIPPED

The 'the login page shows an OTP verification form' step (which checks #step-otp.active with 30s timeout) comes AFTER the MailHog step. Since the MailHog step goes pending, the OTP form check is skipped entirely.

This means the most important assertion in Scenario 1 — that the auth service OTP form actually appears after the email is submitted — is NEVER executed without MailHog. The spec acceptance criteria for ePDS-nr36.7 incorrectly stated 'Scenario 1 gets through the login page shows an OTP verification form before hitting a pending MailHog step' — this is impossible with the current feature file step order.

The test results confirm this: 2 passed (Scenarios 4 and 5), 4 pending (Scenarios 1, 2, 3, 6). Scenario 1 passes steps 1-4 then goes pending on the MailHog step, never reaching the OTP form check.

The feature file step order needs to be corrected: 'the login page shows an OTP verification form' should come BEFORE 'an OTP email arrives in the mail trap'. This is also more accurate to the actual flow (the OTP form appears immediately after email submission; the email arrives asynchronously).

title: Fix: Scenarios 4 and 5 send real OTP emails to alice@example.com on every test run — no test isolation (from ePDS-nr36)
description:
Review of ePDS-nr36 found: The 'the user requests an OTP for {string}' step (auth.steps.ts lines 89-100) does NOT call skipIfNoMailhog(). It navigates to the demo app, submits alice@example.com, and waits for the OTP form. This triggers the auth service to send a real OTP email to alice@example.com on every test run.

Scenarios 4 (wrong code rejection) and 5 (lockout after 5 attempts) both use this step and run fully without MailHog. Every pnpm test:e2e invocation sends 2 OTP emails to alice@example.com (one per scenario). Over time this accumulates and could:
1. Trigger email rate limiting on the Railway SMTP service
2. Pollute the alice@example.com inbox with test emails
3. Cause confusion when debugging (stale OTP tokens from previous runs)

The test email address should use a dedicated test domain (e.g., alice@test.example.com or a Railway-specific address) that is clearly not a real inbox, or the step should use a unique email per run (e.g., alice+{timestamp}@example.com) to avoid token collisions.

title: Create e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json for test TypeScript
description:
## Files
- e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json (create)

## What to do
Create e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json. This is needed because the root tsconfig.json is a composite project-references config that only covers packages/. The e2e/ directory TypeScript files need their own config.

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "outDir": "../.e2e-dist",
    "rootDir": "..",
    "types": ["node"]
  },
  "include": [
    "support/**/*.ts",
    "step-definitions/**/*.ts"
  ]
}
```

Key details:
- Use `NodeNext` for both module and moduleResolution (matches repo convention)
- The `outDir` is `../.e2e-dist` (relative to e2e/) — tsx doesn't actually emit files there, but TypeScript needs it set
- The `rootDir` is `.." so TypeScript can resolve imports correctly
- Do NOT add this tsconfig to the root tsconfig.json references array — it's standalone, not part of the composite build
- Import paths in e2e/*.ts files should use .js extensions per repo convention
- The TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH env var in the test:e2e script already points to this file (set in task .1)

## Don't
- Don't modify tsconfig.json (the root composite config)
- Don't add e2e/ to any existing tsconfig
- Don't set moduleResolution to 'bundler' — must be NodeNext for consistency
- Don't put this file at the repo root — it goes in e2e/
close_reason: 4c654bf Add e2e/tsconfig.e2e.json for test TypeScript

title: Create e2e/support/env.ts — test environment config
description:
## Files
- e2e/support/env.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the environment configuration module that all step definitions and hooks import. It reads from environment variables with Railway defaults.

```ts
export const testEnv = {
  pdsUrl: process.env.E2E_PDS_URL ?? 'https://karmas-e2e-pds.up.railway.app',
  authUrl: process.env.E2E_AUTH_URL ?? 'https://certified-e2e-auth.up.railway.app',
  demoUrl: process.env.E2E_DEMO_URL ?? 'https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app',
  mailhogUrl: process.env.E2E_MAILHOG_URL ?? '',
  headless: process.env.E2E_HEADLESS === 'true',
}
```

Key details:
- `headless` defaults to `false` (headed) — only true when E2E_HEADLESS is explicitly 'true'. This is intentional: the developer wants to see the browser.
- `mailhogUrl` defaults to empty string. When empty, MailHog-dependent steps will call `this.pending()` and skip. When set (e.g. 'http://localhost:8025'), those steps execute.
- All URLs should NOT have trailing slashes.
- This file has no side effects — it's a pure config export.
- Use .js extension in any imports from this file (e.g. `import { testEnv } from './env.js'`) per repo convention.

## Don't
- Don't add any Railway secrets or API keys
- Don't add Docker-local defaults (pds.test, auth.pds.test) — Railway-first
- Don't import anything from the monorepo packages (e2e/ is standalone)
close_reason: 806f838 Create e2e/support/env.ts — test environment config

title: Create e2e/support/world.ts — Cucumber World class with Playwright page
description:
## Files
- e2e/support/world.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the custom Cucumber World class that holds the Playwright page and provides helpers. This is the central object available as `this` in all step definitions.

```ts
import { World, type IWorldOptions, setWorldConstructor } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import type { BrowserContext, Page } from '@playwright/test'
import { testEnv } from './env.js'

export class EpdsWorld extends World {
  declare context: BrowserContext
  declare page: Page

  constructor(options: IWorldOptions) {
    super(options)
  }

  get env() {
    return testEnv
  }

  /**
   * Call in any step that requires MailHog. If E2E_MAILHOG_URL is not set,
   * marks the step as pending and cucumber-js skips remaining steps in the scenario.
   * When MailHog is available, this is a no-op and the step executes normally.
   */
  skipIfNoMailhog(): void {
    if (!testEnv.mailhogUrl) {
      return this.pending()
    }
  }
}

setWorldConstructor(EpdsWorld)
```

Key details:
- `context` and `page` are set by the Before hook in hooks.ts (not in the constructor). Use `declare` to tell TypeScript they exist without initializing them.
- The browser instance is NOT on the World — it's a module-level variable in hooks.ts. This is because BeforeAll and Before have different `this` contexts in cucumber-js. BeforeAll's `this` is NOT a World instance.
- `this.pending()` is a cucumber-js method that throws the magic string 'pending'. Cucumber catches it and marks the step as pending (yellow in reports), then skips remaining steps in the scenario. It does NOT mark the scenario as failed.
- `setWorldConstructor(EpdsWorld)` must be called at module level so cucumber-js uses our World class.
- Step definitions type `this` as `EpdsWorld`: `Given('...', async function(this: EpdsWorld) { ... })`

## Don't
- Don't store `browser: Browser` on the World class (BeforeAll can't set it — different `this`)
- Don't import Browser from playwright — only BrowserContext and Page are needed on the World
- Don't call `this.skip()` — that method doesn't exist in cucumber-js. Use `this.pending()`
- Don't make skipIfNoMailhog async — pending() is synchronous (it throws)
close_reason: 5646bc9 Create e2e/support/world.ts — Cucumber World class with Playwright page

title: Create e2e/support/hooks.ts — browser lifecycle and screenshot on failure
description:
## Files
- e2e/support/hooks.ts (create)

## What to do
Create the Cucumber hooks file that manages the Playwright browser lifecycle and captures screenshots on failure.

```ts
import { BeforeAll, AfterAll, Before, After, setDefaultTimeout } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import { chromium, type Browser } from '@playwright/test'
import { mkdir } from 'node:fs/promises'
import type { EpdsWorld } from './world.js'
import { testEnv } from './env.js'

// Railway services have cold-start latency. Default cucumber step timeout
// is 5 seconds which is too short for OAuth redirect chains.
setDefaultTimeout(60_000)

let sharedBrowser: Browser

BeforeAll(async function () {
  await mkdir('reports/screenshots', { recursive: true })
  sharedBrowser = await chromium.launch({ headless: testEnv.headless })
})

Before(async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  this.context = await sharedBrowser.newContext()
  this.page = await this.context.newPage()
  this.page.setDefaultNavigationTimeout(30_000)
  this.page.setDefaultTimeout(15_000)
})

After(async function (this: EpdsWorld, scenario) {
  if (scenario.result?.status === 'FAILED') {
    const safeName = scenario.pickle.name
      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/gi, '-')
      .replace(/^-|-$/g, '')
      .toLowerCase()
      .slice(0, 100)
    await this.page?.screenshot({
      path: \`reports/screenshots/\${safeName}.png\`,
      fullPage: true,
    })
  }
  await this.context?.close()
})

AfterAll(async function () {
  await sharedBrowser?.close()
})
```

Key details:
- `sharedBrowser` is a MODULE-LEVEL variable, NOT a World property. BeforeAll's `this` is not a World instance.
- `setDefaultTimeout(60_000)` — 60 seconds per step. Railway cold starts + OAuth redirect chains can take 10-20 seconds.
- Each scenario gets its own BrowserContext (isolated cookies, storage, auth state).
- Screenshots are saved on FAILURE only, with sanitized filenames.
- `mkdir('reports/screenshots', { recursive: true })` in BeforeAll ensures the directory exists.

## Don't
- Don't store browser on the World class
- Don't set timeout lower than 30 seconds — Railway latency will cause flakes
- Don't close the browser in After (only close context) — browser is shared across scenarios
close_reason: 2583657 Add e2e/support/hooks.ts — browser lifecycle and screenshot on failure

title: Create e2e/step-definitions/common.steps.ts — shared Background steps
description:
## Files
- e2e/step-definitions/common.steps.ts (create)

## What to do
Implement the Background steps that appear in passwordless-authentication.feature (and most other feature files). These run before every scenario.

Steps to implement:

### 1. `Given the ePDS test environment is running`
Make an HTTP GET to `{testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health` and assert the response status is 200. This confirms the Railway PDS is reachable before the scenario runs. Use the global `fetch` (Node 18+ built-in).

If the health check fails, throw an error with a clear message like 'PDS health check failed: {status} at {url}'.

### 2. `Given a demo OAuth client is registered`
No-op. The Railway demo app is always registered as an OAuth client via its /client-metadata.json endpoint.

### 3. `Given {string} already has a PDS account`
For now, call `this.skipIfNoMailhog()` — creating accounts requires the full OTP flow which needs MailHog.

```ts
import { Given } from '@cucumber/cucumber'
import type { EpdsWorld } from '../support/world.js'
import { testEnv } from '../support/env.js'

Given('the ePDS test environment is running', async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  const res = await fetch(\`\${testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health\`)
  if (!res.ok) {
    throw new Error(\`PDS health check failed: \${res.status} at \${testEnv.pdsUrl}/xrpc/_health\`)
  }
})

Given('a demo OAuth client is registered', async function (this: EpdsWorld) {
  // No-op: Railway demo app is always registered via /client-metadata.json
})

Given('{string} already has a PDS account', async function (this: EpdsWorld, _email: string) {
  this.skipIfNoMailhog()
})
```

Key details:
- Every step function must use `function(this: EpdsWorld)` syntax, NOT arrow functions.
- Import paths use .js extension: `'../support/world.js'`, `'../support/env.js'`
- The health check uses global `fetch` — no need to import it (Node 18+)

## Don't
- Don't use arrow functions for step definitions
- Don't import from monorepo packages (@certified-app/*)
- Don't make the health check retry on failure — fail fast
close_reason: 4b595e6 feat(e2e): add common Background step definitions

title: Add .gitignore entries and verify full e2e run
description:
## Files
- .gitignore (modify)

## What to do
Add gitignore entries for e2e artifacts, then run the full test suite to verify everything works end-to-end.

### 1. Add to .gitignore:
```
# E2E test artifacts
reports/
.e2e-dist/
```

### 2. Verify the full run:
```bash
pnpm install
npx playwright install chromium
pnpm test:e2e
```

### Expected behavior:
A Chromium browser window opens (headed mode). For each scenario in passwordless-authentication.feature:

**Scenario 1 (New user authenticates):**
- Browser navigates to https://certified-appdemo-production.up.railway.app
- The demo login page loads with an email input
- Email 'alice@example.com' is entered and form submitted
- Browser follows OAuth redirect chain to auth service
- OTP form appears on auth service (step passes)
- Next step 'an OTP email arrives in the mail trap' → PENDING (no MailHog)
- Remaining steps skipped

**Scenario 2 (Returning user):**
- Background step 'alice already has a PDS account' → PENDING (needs MailHog)
- Remaining steps skipped

**Scenarios 3-5 (OTP config, wrong code, lockout):**
- Various steps go PENDING

**Scenario 6 (Refresh page):**
- Browser navigates to demo app, page refreshes, login page renders normally
- 'OTP flow still works to completion' → PENDING

### Expected output:
- 0 failures (red)
- Multiple pending steps (yellow)
- Some passing steps (green)
- HTML report generated at reports/e2e.html
- No 'Undefined step' errors

### 3. Also verify:
- `pnpm typecheck` still passes
- `pnpm test` (unit tests) still passes

## Don't
- Don't commit reports/ or screenshots
- Don't modify any .feature files
- Don't change any existing test configuration
close_reason: 59987d7 Add .gitignore entries and fix e2e run

title: Doc comment in complete.ts has duplicate step label '5c' and wrong step reference in comment '6'
description: Review of 0962a39 found: The file-level JSDoc block in complete.ts (lines 18-20) has two entries labeled '5c': one for 'Existing user, needs consent' and one for 'Existing user, no consent needed'. The second should be '5d'. Additionally, line 20 says 'only for 5a random and 5d paths' but the inline comment at line 148 still says 'Step 5b: Redirect to consent screen' (should be 5c). These mismatches make the flow numbering inconsistent and confusing for future maintainers. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts lines 18-20 and line 148.

title: VALID_HANDLE_MODES constant and HandleMode type defined inside request handler scope
description: Review of 0962a39 found: In login-page.ts lines 50-61, VALID_HANDLE_MODES (a const array) and HandleMode (a type alias) are declared inside the router.get() handler body, meaning they are re-created on every request. Per AGENTS.md conventions, module-level magic values should be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE constants at module scope. Moving these outside the handler avoids repeated allocation and aligns with the codebase style. The HandleMode type should also be exported from a shared location (or at minimum from the route file) so complete.ts can use it for the flow.handleMode comparison instead of comparing against a raw string literal. Evidence: packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts lines 50-61.

title: Fix: email-delivery.feature Scenario 2 uses 'the user requests an OTP for' without Mailpit guard (from ePDS-geiz)
description:
Review of ePDS-geiz found: email-delivery.feature Scenario 2 ('Returning user receives a sign-in OTP email') uses:
  When the user requests an OTP for "bob@example.com"

The step definition in auth.steps.ts (line 122) does NOT check testEnv.mailpitPass before navigating to the demo URL and waiting for #step-otp.active. This means:
1. The step will attempt a full browser navigation even when Mailpit is not configured.
2. The subsequent 'an email arrives in the mail trap addressed to' step DOES guard Mailpit, but by then the browser has already navigated and the OTP has been sent to a real email address.

The Background step 'a mail trap is capturing outbound emails' (email.steps.ts line 85) calls skipIfNoMailpit() which returns 'pending' when Mailpit is not configured. With strict: false in cucumber.mjs, this marks the scenario as pending and skips remaining steps. So in practice the scenario IS skipped when Mailpit is absent.

However, the 'the user requests an OTP for {string}' step is also used in passwordless-authentication.feature where Mailpit IS required. The inconsistency is that this step does not self-guard, relying on the Background to gate the scenario.

This is a latent risk: if the step is used in a scenario without a Mailpit-gating Background, it will fire a real OTP send. Consider adding a Mailpit guard to the step itself, or documenting the dependency clearly.
close_reason: 936b11d fix: add Mailpit guard to 'requests an OTP for' step definitions

title: Rename MAGIC_CALLBACK_SECRET → EPDS_CALLBACK_SECRET and /oauth/magic-callback → /oauth/epds-callback
description:
