title: Fix: client background_color override broken by body background in light mode (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The client branding `background_color` override is applied as `background` on the `<html>` element via `rootStyle`, but the `body` has an explicit `background: white` rule inside `@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)` that takes precedence (body > html for background painting). As a result, any client-supplied `background_color` is silently ignored in light mode. Evidence: login-page.ts line 335-337 sets `body { background: white; }` in a light-mode media query, overriding the html-level inline style. Fix: apply the `background_color` override directly to `body` via a `<style>` block or move the override to a CSS custom property consumed by body.
close_reason: 2bc9fac fix background_color override: move from html inline style to body-targeted style block after light-mode media query

title: Fix: --color-text-light uses wrong contrast step (contrast-600 vs spec contrast-700) (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The semantic token `--color-text-light` is set to `var(--color-contrast-600)` (43.6% lightness) but the atproto reference style.css (line 232) defines it as `var(--color-contrast-700)` (34.2% lightness). This makes secondary text (labels, subtitles, placeholder text) slightly too light, reducing contrast and diverging from the reference design. Evidence: login-page.ts line 297 vs atproto style.css line 232. Fix: change `--color-text-light: var(--color-contrast-600)` to `--color-text-light: var(--color-contrast-700)`.
close_reason: 2bc9fac fix: --color-text-light uses contrast-700 per atproto spec

title: Fix: social login buttons remain visible during OTP step (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The social login buttons (Google/GitHub) and their surrounding dividers are rendered outside the step-email/step-otp divs and are never hidden when the user transitions to the OTP step. When a user clicks 'Continue with email' and moves to the OTP step, the social buttons remain visible above the OTP form, which is confusing and inconsistent with the atproto reference UI. Evidence: login-page.ts lines 708-709 — `${socialButtonsHtml}` is placed unconditionally in the form-panel, outside both step divs. Fix: either wrap socialButtonsHtml in a div with class `step-email` so it hides with the email step, or hide it explicitly in `showOtpStep()` / show it in `showEmailStep()`.
close_reason: 2d58468 fix: hide social login buttons during OTP step

title: Fix: success admonition uses wrong icon (alert triangle instead of checkmark) (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The admonition element (#error-msg) always renders an alert triangle SVG icon regardless of whether it is showing an error or a success message. When `showSuccess('Code resent!')` is called, the element switches to the `.success` CSS class (green border/background) but the icon remains the warning triangle, which is semantically incorrect and visually jarring. Evidence: login-page.ts lines 699-706 — the alert triangle SVG is hardcoded inside the admonition div. Fix: either use two separate icon spans (one for error, one for success) toggled by JS, or replace the icon SVG via JS in `showError()`/`showSuccess()`.
close_reason: 2d58468 fix: success admonition shows checkmark icon instead of alert triangle

title: Fix: color-mix() used for admonition backgrounds has limited browser support (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The admonition error/success background uses `color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-error) 8%, transparent)` (login-page.ts lines 649, 655). `color-mix()` is not supported in Safari < 16.4 (March 2023) or Firefox < 113 (May 2023). Users on older browsers will see no background tint on the admonition, which is a minor visual regression but still a compatibility gap. The spec did not require `color-mix()` — it only specified `border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--color-border-default)`. Fix: replace with a hardcoded low-opacity rgba fallback or use a CSS custom property with a pre-computed tint value.
close_reason: a140492 fix: replace color-mix() with rgba fallbacks for admonition backgrounds

title: Fix: justify-content: stretch is invalid in flexbox (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The `.layout` desktop media query sets `justify-content: stretch` (login-page.ts line 352). `stretch` is not a valid value for `justify-content` in flexbox (it is valid for `align-content` and in CSS Grid). Browsers silently ignore this declaration, so the layout falls back to the default `justify-content: flex-start`. This means the right form panel does not expand to fill remaining horizontal space on very wide viewports — the form panel stays at its `max-width: 600px` and the remaining space is empty. Fix: remove `justify-content: stretch` or replace with `justify-content: flex-start` (the default). The left panel's `width: 50%` and the right panel's `width: 100%` already handle the layout correctly without this property.
close_reason: 845b8fa fix: remove invalid justify-content: stretch from flexbox layout

title: Fix: OTP input missing autocomplete='email' on email field (from ePDS-wae.1)
description: Review of ePDS-wae.1 found: The email input (login-page.ts line 722) has `type="email"` but is missing `autocomplete="email"`. Without this attribute, password managers and browser autofill may not correctly identify the field as an email address field, reducing UX especially on mobile. The atproto reference uses `autocomplete` attributes on all form fields. The OTP input already correctly has `autocomplete="one-time-code"`. Fix: add `autocomplete="email"` to the email input element.
close_reason: a97a985 fix: add autocomplete="email" to email input

title: Pixel-perfect polish: align login page CSS with atproto reference
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/login-page.ts (modify) — CSS and HTML tweaks only

## Reference
Compare against the atproto oauth-provider-ui at /home/kzoeps/Projects/atproto/packages/oauth/oauth-provider-ui/

## What to do

The current implementation captures the overall design language but has several pixel-level differences from the atproto reference. Fix all of the following:

### 1. Title panel — desktop layout needs grid centering
The atproto LayoutTitlePage uses a grid inside the left panel to vertically center the content:
\`\`\`css
/* atproto: .title-panel inner wrapper */
display: grid;
align-content: center;
justify-items: end; /* right-aligned on desktop */
\`\`\`
Currently our .title-panel uses flexbox with justify-content:center which doesn't behave the same way. The inner content (logo + h1 + subtitle) should be wrapped in a div with \`display: grid; align-content: center; justify-items: end;\` on desktop. On mobile, the title-panel should use \`justify-items: start\`.

### 2. Title font size — desktop should go larger at lg breakpoint
atproto uses responsive title sizing:
- Mobile: \`text-xl\` = 20px ✓ (we have this)
- md (768px): \`text-2xl\` = 24px (we jump to 40px — too big)
- lg (1024px): \`text-5xl\` = 48px

Fix: Use 24px at md breakpoint, 48px at lg breakpoint (add a 1024px media query).

### 3. Title panel padding
atproto uses:
- Mobile: \`px-6 pt-4\` = padding: 16px 24px (top only, no bottom) ✓ roughly matches
- Desktop: \`p-4\` = 16px all around (we use 32px — too much)

Fix: Desktop padding should be 16px, not 32px.

### 4. Form panel padding
atproto uses:
- Mobile: \`p-6\` = 24px ✓ (we have this)
- Desktop: \`px-12\` = 48px horizontal, but the vertical padding is inherited from p-6 = 24px

Our desktop padding is 48px all around. Fix: desktop should be \`padding: 24px 48px;\`

### 5. Input container — cursor-text class
atproto's InputContainer has \`cursor-text\` on the outer div (so clicking anywhere in the pill focuses the input). Add \`cursor: text;\` to \`.input-container\`.

### 6. Input inner padding
atproto uses \`px-1\` = 4px horizontal padding on the inner flex row. We have \`padding: 0 4px\` which matches. But the icon uses \`mx-2\` = 8px margin on each side. Our icon has \`width: 36px\` as a fixed width. Change to: remove the fixed width, add \`margin: 0 8px;\` to the icon instead.

### 7. Input text styling
atproto's input has: \`text-ellipsis bg-clip-padding\`. Add \`text-overflow: ellipsis;\` and \`background-clip: padding-box;\` to \`.input-field\`.

### 8. Placeholder color in dark mode
atproto uses \`dark:placeholder-gray-500\` which is \`rgb(107 114 128)\`. Our placeholder uses \`var(--color-text-light)\` which is the contrast-700 step. These are close but not identical. Add a dark mode override: \`.input-field::placeholder\` in dark mode should be \`color: rgb(107 114 128);\`

### 9. Button tracking (letter-spacing)
atproto buttons have \`tracking-wide\` = \`letter-spacing: 0.025em;\`. Add this to both \`.btn-primary\` and \`.btn-secondary\`.

### 10. Button touch-manipulation
atproto buttons have \`touch-manipulation\` to eliminate 300ms tap delay on mobile. Add \`touch-action: manipulation;\` to both button classes.

### 11. Form gap should be 16px between fields, 16px before button row
atproto's FormCard uses \`gap-4\` (16px) between the children wrapper and the button row. Our \`.form-group\` has \`gap: 16px\` ✓. But the children wrapper inside uses \`space-y-4\` (16px between fields). Verify our \`.field\` spacing matches — currently \`.field\` has \`gap: 4px\` which is the gap between label and input, not between fields. The gap between fields comes from \`.form-group\`'s gap. This should be correct.

### 12. Button row — exact atproto pattern
atproto uses: \`flex flex-row-reverse flex-wrap items-center justify-end space-x-2 space-x-reverse\`
This translates to:
\`\`\`css
display: flex;
flex-direction: row-reverse;
flex-wrap: wrap;
align-items: center;
justify-content: flex-end;
gap: 8px; /* space-x-2 with space-x-reverse */
\`\`\`
Our current \`.btn-row\` uses \`justify-content: flex-start\` — but since it's row-reverse, flex-start means the right side. Actually this is equivalent to justify-end in a normal row. Verify this is correct. The key difference: atproto uses \`space-x-2 space-x-reverse\` not gap. In practice with our plain CSS, \`gap: 8px\` is fine.

### 13. Subtitle text on the title panel
In atproto, the subtitle says "Enter your username and password" or "Enter your password" etc. Our subtitle says "to use **AppName**". This is fine — it's our own copy. But the subtitle should have \`max-width: 320px\` on desktop (atproto uses \`max-w-xs\` = 320px). We have 280px — change to 320px.

### 14. Logo margin on desktop
atproto: \`mb-2\` on mobile (8px), \`md:mb-4\` (16px) on desktop. We have \`margin-bottom: 8px\` on desktop. Change to 16px.

### 15. Title margin on desktop
atproto: \`md:my-4\` = 16px top and bottom margin on desktop. We have \`margin: 16px 0\` ✓. This matches.

### 16. Body font — ensure system font stack matches
atproto uses Tailwind's default system font stack which is:
\`ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Noto Color Emoji"\`
We use: \`-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif\`
These are functionally equivalent but for pixel-perfection, use the Tailwind stack.

## Don't
- Do NOT change any JavaScript logic
- Do NOT change form field names, IDs, or data flow
- Do NOT add external dependencies
- Do NOT modify any other files
- Do NOT change the renderError() function
close_reason: c29d20f polish: align login page CSS with atproto reference

title: Fail fast on missing PDS_INTERNAL_URL and EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET in auth routes
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/complete.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug/security issue at choose-handle.ts:62-64 and complete.ts:91-92.

**Original comments:**
> Falling back to `ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl` and `""` turns a startup misconfiguration into public-network calls or guaranteed 401/403s from every internal endpoint. Require the internal URL and secret here instead of silently booting in a broken state.

Both files have the same pattern:
```ts
const pdsUrl = process.env.PDS_INTERNAL_URL || ctx.config.pdsPublicUrl
const internalSecret = process.env.EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET ?? ""
```

Replace with:
```ts
const pdsUrl = process.env.PDS_INTERNAL_URL
const internalSecret = process.env.EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET
if (!pdsUrl || !internalSecret) {
  throw new Error("PDS_INTERNAL_URL and EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET must be set")
}
```

**Context:**
- In choose-handle.ts these vars are read at router-creation time (line 62-64, inside `createChooseHandleRouter`), so the throw happens at startup. Good.
- In complete.ts these vars are read inside the request handler (line 91-92). Move them to the top-level scope of `createCompleteRouter` (or equivalent factory function) so the throw happens at startup, not at first request.
- Keep `handleDomain = ctx.config.pdsHostname` as-is in choose-handle.ts.
- Per AGENTS.md: "Auth service must use PDS_INTERNAL_URL to reach pds-core over the internal network."

## Dont
- Dont change any other env var reads or fallback patterns outside these two files
- Dont modify the request handlers logic beyond moving the env var reads to startup scope in complete.ts
close_reason: bdffa33 fail fast on missing PDS_INTERNAL_URL and EPDS_INTERNAL_SECRET in auth routes

title: Remove premature auth_flow cleanup from choose-handle POST
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at choose-handle.ts:295-297.

**Original comment:**
> pds-core redirects back to `/auth/choose-handle?error=handle_taken` when the final `createAccount()` collides. Deleting the `auth_flow` row and cookie here turns that recovery path into an immediate "Session expired". Let TTL cleanup handle it, or clear the flow only after the callback definitively succeeds.

Currently at lines 295-297:
```ts
// Step 6: Cleanup — delete auth_flow row and clear cookie
ctx.db.deleteAuthFlow(flowId)
res.clearCookie(AUTH_FLOW_COOKIE)
```

**Fix:** Remove these two lines entirely. The auth_flow table has a 10-minute TTL (`AUTH_FLOW_TTL_MS`), `getAuthFlow()` already filters on `expires_at > now`, and `cleanupExpiredAuthFlows()` runs every 5 minutes in `AuthServiceContext`. Stale rows self-expire safely.

Also update the JSDoc comment at the top of the file (lines 11-15) which currently says:
```
b. Deletes auth_flow row + clears cookie (deferred cleanup from complete.ts)
```
Change to something like:
```
b. Auth_flow row is left intact for handle_taken retry — TTL cleanup handles expiry
```

And update the comment on line 14-15 similarly.

**Context:** When pds-core encounters a handle collision during `createAccount()`, it redirects back to `/auth/choose-handle?error=handle_taken`. If the auth_flow row and cookie are already deleted, `getFlowAndSession()` fails immediately with "Session expired" and the user cannot retry with a different handle.

## Dont
- Dont add recordClientLogin here — that is deferred to the consent-on-signup PR
- Dont change the deleteAuthFlow/clearCookie in complete.ts (line 129-130) — that path is for existing users and is correct
- Dont modify the redirect URL or HMAC signing logic
close_reason: bdffa33 Remove premature auth_flow cleanup from choose-handle POST

title: Fail closed on handle lookup errors in pds-core check-handle endpoint
description:
## Files
- packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at pds-core/index.ts:466-470.

**Original comment:**
> Returning `{ exists: false }` from the catch makes transient account-manager failures look like an available handle. The auth-service will then approve a handle that cannot actually be created.

Currently at lines 466-470:
```ts
try {
  const account = await pds.ctx.accountManager.getAccount(handle)
  res.json({ exists: !!account })
} catch {
  res.json({ exists: false })
}
```

**Fix:** Change the catch block to fail closed:
```ts
} catch (err) {
  logger.error({ err, handle }, "Failed to check handle availability")
  res.status(503).json({ error: "handle_check_failed" })
}
```

**Context:** This is the `/_internal/check-handle` endpoint called by auth-service to verify handle availability. If the PDS account manager throws (e.g. SQLite busy, corrupt DB), returning `{ exists: false }` tells the auth-service the handle is available, leading to a `createAccount` failure later with a confusing error.

## Dont
- Dont change the happy path (the try block)
- Dont change the request validation above (handle param check)
- Dont modify any other internal endpoints
close_reason: f2edd4b fail closed on handle lookup errors in check-handle endpoint

title: Validate hex format before timingSafeEqual in verifyCallback
description:
## Files
- packages/shared/src/crypto.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at shared/src/crypto.ts:118-122.

**Original comment:**
> The length check `sig.length === expected.length` only compares string lengths, not hex validity. A 64-character string containing non-hex characters will pass this check but truncate when decoded with `Buffer.from(sig, "hex")`, causing `crypto.timingSafeEqual()` to throw when the resulting buffer length mismatches.

Currently at lines 116-122:
```ts
// Both are hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 (always 64 chars / 32 bytes).
// Guard against wrong-length input to keep timingSafeEqual happy.
if (sig.length !== expected.length) return false
return crypto.timingSafeEqual(
  Buffer.from(expected, "hex"),
  Buffer.from(sig, "hex"),
)
```

**Fix:** Replace the string-length check with a hex-format regex:
```ts
const normalizedSig = sig.toLowerCase()
if (!/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/.test(normalizedSig)) return false
const isValid = crypto.timingSafeEqual(
  Buffer.from(expected, "hex"),
  Buffer.from(normalizedSig, "hex"),
)
return isValid
```

The regex validates: exactly 64 hex chars (32 bytes = HMAC-SHA256 output), lowercase. This prevents Buffer.from truncation on non-hex input and ensures buffer lengths always match for timingSafeEqual.

**Context:** This is the `verifyCallback()` function used on the public `/oauth/epds-callback` route. A malformed `sig` query parameter could cause an unhandled exception, crashing the request.

## Dont
- Dont change the signCallback function
- Dont change the HMAC payload construction (lines 103-114)
- Dont change the timestamp validation above (lines 95-101)
close_reason: f2edd4b validate hex format before timingSafeEqual in verifyCallback

title: Render submit button enabled by default in choose-handle page
description:
## Files
- packages/auth-service/src/routes/choose-handle.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at choose-handle.ts:444-445 and 470-472.

**Original comment:**
> The button starts disabled, and only the inline availability script can enable it. If that script is blocked or throws, signup becomes impossible even though the POST handler already re-validates the handle server-side. Render the button enabled by default and let JS opt into disabling once it has loaded.

**Fix 1 — HTML (line 444):**
Change:
```html
<button type="submit" id="submit-btn" class="btn-primary" disabled>Continue</button>
```
To:
```html
<button type="submit" id="submit-btn" class="btn-primary">Continue</button>
```

**Fix 2 — JavaScript (around line 470-472):**
The `updateSubmit()` function already exists and sets `submitBtn.disabled = !isAvailable`. Add a call to `updateSubmit()` right after the function definition so JS immediately disables the button once loaded (progressive enhancement pattern):
```js
function updateSubmit() {
  submitBtn.disabled = !isAvailable;
}
updateSubmit();  // <-- add this line
```

This way: without JS the button works (server validates). With JS, the button starts disabled until availability is confirmed.

## Dont
- Dont change the server-side POST validation logic
- Dont change the availability check fetch logic
- Dont change any CSS classes or styling
close_reason: f2edd4b fix: render submit button enabled by default in choose-handle page

title: Double OTP send: /oauth/authorize creates new auth_flow on every GET, needs idempotency for reused request_uri
description:
During testing, two OTP emails were sent for a single login attempt. The initial hypothesis was that the StayFocusd browser extension was making a duplicate GET request to /oauth/authorize, triggering a second auth_flow and a second OTP send.

However, the root cause may be different: the login flow is initiated by the client app making a GET request to /oauth/authorize, and it is possible that:
1. The request from the client app to initiate the login flow should be a POST, not a GET
2. ePDS should reject GET requests to /oauth/authorize and only accept POST

If (1) is true, StayFocusd following the GET redirect URL would be harmless (since ePDS would reject it). If (2) is already enforced and was not the issue, the root cause is elsewhere.

The duplicate request that was observed came from a different IP and user-agent than the real browser. This is consistent with StayFocusd, but could also be another extension, a proxy, or a prefetch mechanism.

## Investigation needed

The Mermaid sequence diagrams (ePDS-y1l) will clarify exactly what HTTP method is used at each step of both flows and should be reviewed before drawing conclusions.

Questions to answer:
- What HTTP method does the AT Protocol spec require for the authorization endpoint redirect?
- Does ePDS currently accept GET, POST, or both for /oauth/authorize?
- Is the duplicate request coming from before or after the PAR redirect?
- If the fix is to require POST, what changes are needed in the client app (maearth-demo)?

## Observed symptom
- Two OTP emails sent to the same address for a single login attempt
- Duplicate request from different IP/user-agent than the real browser
- Only observed with StayFocusd extension installed; not reproduced in incognito
notes:
AT Protocol spec investigation complete: GET is the correct HTTP method for /oauth/authorize. The browser redirect to the authorization endpoint is inherently a GET per the OAuth 2.0 / PAR spec (RFC 9126). Requiring POST would be spec-violating.

The real issue: /oauth/authorize creates a new auth_flow row on every GET request, regardless of whether the request_uri has already been processed. If StayFocusd (or any other agent) makes a second GET to the same /oauth/authorize?request_uri=... URL, a second auth_flow is created and a second OTP is sent.

Correct fix: /oauth/authorize should be idempotent for a given request_uri. If a request_uri has already been consumed (or has an existing auth_flow), the second GET should either:
(a) Return the existing auth_flow / OTP step without sending a new OTP, or
(b) Return a 400 error rejecting the reused request_uri.

This is an atproto/oauth-provider behaviour question — need to check what the library does and whether there is a hook to intercept duplicate requests.
close_reason: Fixed by deduplicating GET /oauth/authorize on request_uri. getAuthFlowByRequestUri() added to MagicPdsDb; handler reuses existing flow and logs a warn with IP/UA when a duplicate is detected. New PAR calls always produce a fresh request_uri so unrelated logins and the Resend button are unaffected.

title: Fix: res.locals.did typed as string but set from result.did which could be null (from ePDS-01p.3)
description:
Review of ePDS-01p.3 found: in requireBetterAuth middleware (account-settings.ts line 80):
  res.locals.did = result.did

At this point result.did is guaranteed non-null (the null branch redirects at line 54-76). However, all handler call sites cast it as:
  const did = res.locals.did as string

This is an unsafe cast — res.locals is typed as Record<string, any> so TypeScript cannot verify the guarantee. If the middleware logic ever changes, handlers would silently receive null and pass it to DB operations (getBackupEmails, addBackupEmail, removeBackupEmail, deleteAccountData) without any runtime error until the DB operation fails.

Additionally, line 123 has a dead-code fallback:
  did: did ?? '(unknown)'
This is unreachable because did is already cast as string (never null/undefined at that point), but it suggests the author was uncertain about the guarantee.

Fix: either (a) add a runtime assertion in the middleware before calling next(), or (b) use a typed locals interface via Express declaration merging to make the guarantee explicit at the type level. At minimum, remove the dead did ?? '(unknown)' fallback or replace the as string cast with a proper runtime check.

title: Fix: handle collision redirect missing 303 status code in pds-core (from ePDS-1k2.6)
description:
Review of ePDS-1k2.6 found: The handle collision redirect in packages/pds-core/src/index.ts (line 202) uses `res.redirect(url)` without a status code, defaulting to HTTP 302. All other redirects in this file explicitly use 303 (e.g., lines 298 and 322). After a POST-like operation (account creation attempt), 303 is the correct status to prevent the browser from re-POSTing on back navigation.

Evidence:
- Line 202: `res.redirect(`${authServiceUrl}/auth/choose-handle?error=handle_taken`)` — no status code
- Line 298: `res.redirect(303, redirectUrl.toString())` — correct pattern
- Line 322: `res.redirect(303, errorUrl.toString())` — correct pattern

Fix: Change to `res.redirect(303, ...)`.

title: Fix: btn-google and btn-github CSS classes defined in HTML but never styled (from ePDS-wae.1)

The buttons rely entirely on .btn-social for styling. This means there is no way to differentiate Google vs GitHub button appearance (e.g. brand colors, hover states) without adding these rules. Currently harmless but the classes are dead weight and misleading — either add per-provider styles or remove the classes from the HTML.

title: Fix: missing test coverage for ePDS-wh1.3/wh1.4/wh1.6 new behaviors (from ePDS-wh1)
description:
Review of ePDS-wh1 tasks found three new behaviors with no test coverage:

1. ePDS-wh1.4 (verifyCallback hex validation): The existing test 'rejects wrong-length sig' passes 'tooshort' but there is no test for a 64-character string containing non-hex characters (e.g. 64 x 'Z'). This is the exact attack vector the fix was designed to prevent — a malformed sig that passes the old length check but causes Buffer.from truncation. A test like: expect(verifyCallback(params, ts, 'Z'.repeat(64), secret)).toBe(false) should be added to packages/shared/src/__tests__/crypto.test.ts.

2. ePDS-wh1.3 (check-handle 503): No test covers the new catch branch returning 503 { error: 'handle_check_failed' }. The choose-handle.test.ts has no test for when the PDS check-handle endpoint returns 503 and how the auth-service handles it.

3. ePDS-wh1.6 (ping non-OK response): No test covers the new pingRes.ok check in the choose-handle GET handler. The choose-handle.test.ts has no test for when ping-request returns a non-2xx status (e.g. 404 request_expired) and the handler should return 400 with 'Session expired'.

Evidence: grep -n 'non-hex|uppercase|ABCDEF|handle_check_failed|503|ping|pingRes|request_expired|session expired' packages/shared/src/__tests__/crypto.test.ts packages/auth-service/src/__tests__/choose-handle.test.ts returns no matches.

title: Fix: better-auth.ts re-parses OTP_LENGTH independently instead of using ctx.config.otpLength (from ePDS-ipm)
description:
Review of ePDS-ipm found: createBetterAuth() and runBetterAuthMigrations() both re-read process.env.OTP_LENGTH directly instead of accepting the already-validated otpLength from AuthServiceConfig. This creates a seam where the two can diverge.

Evidence:
- index.ts main() validates config.otpLength (range 4-12) and stores it in AuthServiceConfig
- better-auth.ts createBetterAuth() re-reads process.env.OTP_LENGTH independently (line 149)
- better-auth.ts runBetterAuthMigrations() re-reads process.env.OTP_LENGTH independently (line 84)
- createBetterAuth() is called from createAuthService() which receives a fully-built AuthServiceConfig, but otpLength is never passed through

Divergence scenarios:
1. If OTP_LENGTH='' (empty string): index.ts uses || so defaults to '8'; better-auth.ts uses ?? so passes '' to parseInt → NaN → better-auth gets NaN otpLength (validation in main() doesn't protect this path since createAuthService can be called without main())
2. If createAuthService is called programmatically with a config.otpLength that differs from process.env.OTP_LENGTH, the UI and better-auth will disagree on code length

Fix: Add otpLength parameter to createBetterAuth() and runBetterAuthMigrations(), pass ctx.config.otpLength from index.ts. This makes the config the single source of truth.
close_reason: 1b73865 fix: pass otpLength param to createBetterAuth and runBetterAuthMigrations
